text: And the Lord spoke to Moses in the desert of Sinai in the tabernacle of the covenant, the first day of the second month, the second year of their going out of Egypt, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the sum of all the congregation of the children of Israel by their families, and houses, and the names of every one, as many as are of the male sex,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From twenty years old and upwards, of all the men of Israel fit for war, and you shall number them by their troops, thou and Aaron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be with you the princes of the tribes, and of the houses in their kindreds,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whose names are these: Of Ruben, Elisur the son of Sedeur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Simeon, Salamiel the son of Surisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Juda, Nahasson the son of Aminadab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Issachar, Nathanael the son of Suar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Zabulon, Eliab the son of Helon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the sons of Joseph: of Ephraim, Elisama the son of Ammiud: of Manasses, Gamaliel the son of Phadassur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Benjamin, Abidan the son of Gedeon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Dan, Ahiezer the son of Ammisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Aser, Phegiel the son of Ochran.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Gad, Eliasaph the son of Duel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Nephtali, Ahira the son of Enan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the most noble princes of the multitude by their tribes and kindreds, and the chiefs of the army of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whom Moses and Aaron took with all the multitude of the common people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And assembled them on the first day of the second month, reckoning them up by the kindreds, and houses, and families, and heads, and names of every one from twenty years old and upward,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As the Lord had commanded Moses. And they were numbered in the desert of Sinai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Ruben the eldest son of Israel, by their generations and families and houses and names of every head, all that were of the male sex, from twenty years old and upward, that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Were forty-six thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Simeon by their generations and families, and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names and heads of every one, all that were of the male sex, from twenty years old and upward, that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fifty-nine thousand three hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Gad, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Juda, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds, by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Were reckoned up seventy-four thousand six hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Issachar, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds, by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that could go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Were reckoned up fifty-four thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Zabulon, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fifty-seven thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Joseph, namely, of the sons of Ephraim, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forty thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover of the sons of Manasses, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that could go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thirty-two thousand two hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Benjamin, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thirty-five thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Dan, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sixty-two thousand seven hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Aser, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forty-one thousand and five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the sons of Nephtali, by their generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned up by the names of every one from twenty years old and upward, were able to go forth to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fifty-three thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are they who were numbered by Moses and Aaron, and the twelve princes of Israel, every one by the houses of their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole number of the children of Israel by their houses and families, from twenty years old and upward, that were able to go to war,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Levites in the tribes of their families were not numbered with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Number not the tribe of Levi, neither shalt thou put down the sum of them with the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But appoint them over the tabernacle of the testimony, and all the vessels thereof, and whatsoever pertaineth to the ceremonies. They shall carry the tabernacle and all the furniture thereof: and they shall minister, and shall encamp round about the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you are to go forward, the Levites shall take down the tabernacle: when you are to camp, they shall set it up. What stranger soever cometh to it, shall be slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel shall camp every man by his troops and bands and army.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Levites shall pitch their tents round about the tabernacle, lest there come indignation upon the multitude of the children of Israel, and they shall keep watch, and guard the tabernacle of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel did according to all things which the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the children of Israel shall camp by their troops, ensigns, and standards, and the houses of their kindreds, round about the tabernacle of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On the east Juda shall pitch his tents by the bands of his army: and the prince of his sons; shall be Nahasson the son of Aminadab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole sum of the fighting men of his stock, were seventy-four thousand six hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Next unto him they of the tribe of Issachar encamped, whose prince was Nathanael, the son of Suar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole number of his fighting men were fifty-four thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the tribe of Zabulon the prince was Eliab the son of Helon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the army of fighting men of his stock, were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that were numbered in the camp of Juda, were a hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred: and they by their troops shall march first.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the camp of the sons of Ruben, on the south side, the prince shall be Elisur the son of Sedeur:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole army of his fighting men, that were numbered, were forty-six thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beside him camped they of the tribe of Simeon: whose prince was Salamiel the son of Surisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole army of his fighting men, that were numbered, were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the tribe of Gad the prince was Eliasaph the son of Duel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole army of his fighting men that were numbered, were forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that were reckoned up in the camp of Ruben, were a hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty, by their troops: they shall march in the second place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tabernacle of the testimony shall be carried by the officers of the Levites and their troops. As it shall be set up, so shall it be taken down. Every one shall march according to their places, and ranks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On the west side shall be the camp of the sons of Ephraim, whose prince was Elisama the son of Ammiud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The whole army of his fighting men, that were numbered, were forty thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And with them the tribe of the sons of Manasses, whose prince was Gamaliel the son of Phadassur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole army of his fighting men, that were numbered, were thirty-two thousand two hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the tribe of the sons of Benjamin the prince was Abidan the son of Gedeon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole army of fighting men, that were reckoned up, were thirty-five thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that were numbered in the camp of Ephraim, were a hundred and eight-thousand one hundred by their troops: they shall march in the third place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On the north side camped the sons of Dan: whose prince was Ahiezar the son of Ammisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The whole army of his fighting men, that were numbered, were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beside him they of the tribe of Aser pitched their tents: whose prince was Phegiel the son of Ochran.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The whole army of his fighting men, that were numbered, were forty-one thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of the sons of Nephtali the prince was Ahira the son of Enan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The whole army of his fighting men, were fifty-three thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that were numbered in the camp of Dan, were a hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred: and they shall march last.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the number of the children of Israel, of their army divided according to the houses of their kindreds and their troops, six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Levites were not numbered among the children of Israel: for so the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel did according to all things that the Lord had commanded. They camped by their troops, and marched by the families and houses of their fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the generations of Aaron and Moses in the day that the Lord spoke to Moses in mount Sinai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these the names of the sons of Aaron: his firstborn Nadab, then Abiu, and Eleazar, and Ithamar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These the names of the sons of Aaron the priests that were anointed, and whose hands were filled and consecrated, to do the functions of priesthood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Nadab and Abiu died, without children, when they offered strange fire before the Lord, in the desert of Sinai: and Eleazar and Ithamar performed the priestly office in the presence of Aaron their father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bring the tribe of Levi, and make them stand in the sight of Aaron the priest to minister to him, and let them watch,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And observe whatsoever appertaineth to the service of the multitude before the tabernacle of the testimony,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And let them keep the vessels of the tabernacle, serving in the ministry thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt give the Levites for a gift,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To Aaron and to his sons, to whom they are delivered by the children of Israel. But thou shalt appoint Aaron and his sons over the service of priesthood. The stranger that approacheth to minister, shall be put to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have taken the Levites from the children of Israel, for every firstborn that openeth the womb among the children of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For every firstborn is mine: since I struck the firstborn in the land of Egypt: I have sanctified to myself whatsoever is firstborn in Israel both of man and beast, they are mine: I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses in the desert of Sinai, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Number the sons of Levi by the houses of their fathers and their families, every male from one month and upward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses numbered them as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there were found sons of Levi by their names, Gerson and Caath Merari.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Gerson: Lebni and Semei.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Caath: Amram, and Jesaar, Hebron and Oziel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Merari, Moholi and Musi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Gerson were two families, the Lebnites, and the Semeites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of which were numbered, people of the male sex from one month and upward, seven thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These shall pitch behind the tabernacle on the west,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Under their prince Eliasaph the son of Lael.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their charge shall be in the tabernacle of the covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The tabernacle itself and the cover thereof, the hanging that is drawn before the doors of the tabernacle of the covenant, and the curtains of the court: the hanging also that is hanged in the entry of the court of the tabernacle, and whatsoever belongeth to the rite of the altar, the cords of the tabernacle, and all the furniture thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the kindred of Caath come the families of the Amramites and Jesaarites and Hebronites and Ozielites. These are the families of the Caathites reckoned up by their names:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All of the male sex from one month and upward, eight thousand six hundred: they shall have the guard of the sanctuary,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall camp on the south side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their prince shall be Elisaphan the son of Oziel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall keep the ark, and the table and the candlestick, the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary, wherewith they minister, and the veil, and all the furniture of this kind.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the prince of the princes of the Levites, Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, shall be over them that watch for the guard of the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of Merari are the families of the Moholites, and Musites, reckoned up by their names:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All of the male kind from one month and upward, six thousand two hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their prince Suriel the son of Abihaiel: their shall camp on the north side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Under their custody shall be the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars, and the pillars and their sockets, and all things that pertain to this kind of service:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the pillars of the court round about with their sockets, and the pins with their cords.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Before the tabernacle of the covenant, that is to say on the east side shall Moses and Aaron camp, with their sons, having the custody of the sanctuary, in the midst of the children of Israel. What stranger soever cometh unto it, shall be put to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the Levites, that I Moses and Aaron numbered according to the precept of the Lord, by their f families, of the male kind from one month and upward, were twenty-two thousand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Number the firstborn of the male sex of the children of Israel, from one month and upward, and thou shalt take the sum of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt take the Levites to me for all the firstborn of the children of Israel, I am the Lord: and their cattle for all the firstborn of the cattle of the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses reckoned up, as the Lord had commanded, the firstborn of the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the males by their names, from one month and upward, were twenty-two thousand two hundred and seventy-three.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the Levites for the firstborn of the children of Israel, and the cattle of the Levites for their cattle, and the Levites shall be mine. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But for the price of the two hundred and seventy-three, of the firstborn of the children of Israel, that exceed the number of the Levites,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take five sicles for every head, according to the weight of the sanctuary. A sicle hath twenty obols.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt give the money to Aaron and his sons, the price of them that are above.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore took the money of them that were above, and whom they had redeemed from the Levites,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the firstborn of the children of Israel, one thousand three hundred and sixty-five sicles, according to the weight of the sanctuary,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And gave it to Aaron and his sons according to the word that the Lord had commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the sum of the sons of Caath from the midst of the Levites, by their houses and families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, to fifty years old, of all that go in to stand and to minister in the tabernacle of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the service of the sons of Caath:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the camp is; to set forward, Aaron and his sons shall go into the tabernacle of the covenant, and the holy of holies, and shall take down the veil that hangeth before the door, and shall wrap up the ark of the testimony in it,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall cover it again with a cover of violet skins, and shall spread over it a cloth all of violet, and shall put in the bars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall wrap up also the table of proposition in a cloth of violet, and shall put with it the censers and little mortars, the cups and bowls to pour out the libations: the loaves shall be always on it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall spread over it a cloth of scarlet, which again they shall cover with a covering of violet skins, and shall put in the bars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall take also a cloth of violet wherewith they shall cover the candlestick with the lamps and tongs thereof and the snuffers and all the oil vessels, which are necessary for the dressing of the lamps:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And over all they shall put a cover of violet skins and put in the bars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall wrap up the golden altar also in a cloth of violet, and shall spread over it a cover of violet skins, and put in the bars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the vessels wherewith they minister in the sanctuary, they shall wrap up in a cloth of violet, and shall spread over it a cover of violet skins, and put in the bars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall cleanse the altar also from the ashes, and shall wrap it up in a purple cloth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall put it with all the vessels that they use in the ministry thereof, that is to say, firepans, fleshhooks and forks, pothooks and shovels. They shall cover all the vessels of the altar together with a covering of violet skins, and shall put in the bars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Aaron and his sons have wrapped up the sanctuary and the vessels thereof at the removing of the camp, then shall the sons of Caath enter in to carry the things wrapped up: and they shall not touch the vessels of the sanctuary, lest they die. These are the burdens of the sons of Caath: in the tabernacle of the covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And over them shall be Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, to whose charge pertaineth the oil to dress the lamps, and the sweet incense, and the sacrifice, that is always offered, and the oil of unction, and whatsoever pertaineth to the service of the tabernacle, and of all the vessels that are in the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Destroy not the people of Caath from the midst of the Levites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But do this to them, that they may live, and not die, by touching the holies of holies. Aaron and his sons shall go in, and they shall appoint every man his work, and shall divide the burdens that every man is to carry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let not others by any curiosity see the things that are in the sanctuary before they be wrapped up, otherwise they shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the sum of the sons of Gerson also by their houses and families and kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, unto fifty years old. Number them all that go in and minister in the tabernacle of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the office of the family of the Gersonites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To carry the curtains of the tabernacle and the roof of the covenant, the other covering, and the violet covering over all, and the hanging that hangeth in the entry of the tabernacle of the covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The curtains of the court, and the veil in the entry that is before tabernacle. All things that pertain to the altar, the cords and the vessels of the ministry,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Gerson shall carry, by the commandment of Aaron and his sons: and each man shall know to what burden he must be assigned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the service of the family of the Gersonites in the tabernacle of the covenant, and they shall be under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt reckon up the sons of Merari also by the families and houses of their fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, unto fifty years old, all that go in to the office of their ministry, and to the service of the covenant of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are their burdens: They shall carry the boards of the tabernacle and the bars thereof, the pillars and their sockets,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The pillars also of the court round about, with their sockets and pins and cords. They shall receive by account all the vessels and furniture, and so shall carry them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the office of the family of the Merarites, and their ministry in the tabernacle of the covenant: and they shall be under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Moses and Aaron and the princes of the synagogue reckoned up the sons of Caath, by their kindreds and the houses of their fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, unto fifty years old, all that go in to the ministry of the tabernacle of the covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they were found two thousand seven hundred and fifty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the number of the people of Caath that go in to the tabernacle of the covenant: these did Moses and Aaron number according to the word of the Lord by the hand of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Gerson also were numbered by the kindreds and houses of their fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, unto fifty years old, all that go in to minister in the tabernacle of the covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they were found two thousand six hundred and thirty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the people of the Gersonites, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the word of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Merari also were numbered by the kindreds and houses of their fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, unto fifty years old, all that go in to fulfil the rites of the tabernacle of the covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they were found three thousand two hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the number of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron reckoned up according to the commandment of the Lord by the hand of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that were reckoned up of the Levites, and whom Moses and Aaron and the princes of Israel took by name, by the kindreds and houses of their fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thirty years old and upward, until fifty years old, that go into the ministry of the tabernacle, and to carry the burdens,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Were in all eight thousand five hundred and eighty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses reckoned them up according to the word of the Lord, every one according to their office and burdens, as the Lord had commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel, that they cast out of the camp every leper, and whosoever hath an issue of seed, or is defiled by the dead:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whether it be man or woman, cast ye them out of the camp, lest they defile it when I shall dwell with you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel did so, and they cast them forth without the camp, as the Lord had spoken to Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to the children of Israel: When a man or woman shall have committed any of all the sins that men are wont to commit, and by negligence shall have transgressed the commandment of the Lord, and offended,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall confess their sin, and restore the principal itself, and the fifth part over and above, to him against whom they have sinned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if there be no one to receive it, they shall give it to the Lord, and it shall be the priest’s, besides the ram that is offered for expiation, to be an atoning sacrifice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the firstfruits also, which the children of Israel offer, belong to the priest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whatsoever is offered into the sanctuary by every one, and is delivered into the hands of the priest, it shall be his.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: The man whose wife shall have gone astray, and contemning her husband,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall have slept with another man, and her husband cannot discover it, but the adultery is secret, and cannot be proved by witnesses, because she was not found in the adultery:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the spirit of jealousy stir up the husband against his wife, who either is defiled, or is charged with false suspicion,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall bring her to the priest, and shall offer an oblation for her, the tenth part of a measure of barley meal: he shall not pour oil thereon, nor put frankincense upon it: because it is a sacrifice of jealousy, and an oblation searching out adultery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The priest therefore shall offer it, and set it before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall take holy water in an earthen vessel, and he shall cast a little earth of the pavement of the tabernacle into it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the woman shall stand before the Lord, he shall uncover her head, and shall put on her hands the sacrifice of remembrance, and the oblation of jealousy: and he himself shall hold the most bitter waters, whereon he hath heaped curses with execration.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall adjure her, and shall say: If another man hath not slept with thee, and if thou be not defiled by forsaking thy husband’s bed, these most bitter waters, on which I have heaped curses, shall not hurt thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou hast gone aside from thy husband, and art defiled, and hast lain with another man:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These curses shall light upon thee: The Lord make thee a curse, and an example for all among his people: may he make thy thigh to rot, and may thy belly swell and burst asunder.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the cursed waters enter into thy belly, and may thy womb swell and thy thigh rot. And the woman shall answer, Amen, amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall write these curses in a book, and shall wash them out with the most bitter waters, upon which he hath heaped the curses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall give them her to drink. And when she hath drunk them up,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The priest shall take from her hand the sacrifice of jealousy, and shall elevate it before the Lord, and shall put it upon the altar: yet so as first,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To take a handful of the sacrifice of that which is offered, and burn it upon the altar: and so give the most bitter waters to the woman to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when she hath drunk them, if she be defiled, and having despised her husband be guilty of adultery, the malediction shall go through her, and her belly swelling, her thigh shall rot: and the woman shall be a curse, and an example to all the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if she be not defiled, she shall not be hurt, and shall bear children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of jealousy. If a woman hath gone aside from her husband, and be defiled,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the husband stirred up by the spirit of jealousy bring her before the Lord, and the priest do to her according to all things that are here written:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The husband shall be blameless, and she shall bear her iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When a man, or woman, shall make a vow to be sanctified, and will consecrate themselves to the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall abstain from wine, and from every thing that may make a man drunk. They shall not drink vinegar of wine, or of any other drink, nor any thing that is pressed out of the grape: nor shall they eat grapes either fresh or dried.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the days that they are consecrated to the Lord by vow: they shall eat nothing that cometh of the vineyard, from the raisin even to the kernel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the time of his separation no razor shall pass over his head, until the day be fulfilled of his consecration to the Lord. He shall be holy, and shall let the hair of his head grow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the time of his consecration he shall not go in to any dead,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall he make himself unclean, even for his father, or for his mother, or for his brother, or for his sister, when they die, because the consecration of his God is upon his head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the days of his separation he shall be holy to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if any man die suddenly before him: the head of his consecration shall be defiled: and he shall shave it forthwith on the same day of his purification, and again on the seventh day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons to the priest in the entry of the covenant of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall offer one for sin, and the other for a holocaust, and shall pray for him, for that he hath sinned by the dead: and he shall sanctify his head that day:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall consecrate to the Lord the days of his separation, offering a lamb of one year for sin: yet so that the former days be made void, because his sanctification was profaned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of consecration. When the days which he had determined by vow shall be expired, he shall bring him to the door of the tabernacle of the covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall offer his oblation to the Lord: one he lamb of a year old without blemish for a holocaust, and one ewe lamb of a year old without blemish for a sin offering, and one ram without blemish for a victim of peace offering,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A basket also of unleavened bread, tempered with oil, and wafers without leaven anointed with oil, and the libations of each:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall present them before the Lord, and shall offer both the sin offering and the holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the ram he shall immolate for a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord, offering at the same time the basket of unleavened bread, and the libations that are due by custom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the hair of the consecration of the Nazarite, be shaved off before the door of the tabernacle of the covenant: and he shall take his hair, and lay it upon the fire, which is under the sacrifice of the peace offerings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram, and one unleavened cake out of the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and he shall deliver them into the hands of the Nazarite, after his head is shaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And receiving them again from him, he shall elevate them in the sight of the Lord: and they being sanctified shall belong to the priest, as the breast, which was commanded to be separated, and the shoulder. After this the Nazarite may drink wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of the Nazarite, when he hath vowed his oblation to the Lord in the time of his consecration, besides those things which his hand shall find, according to that which he had vowed in his mind, so shall he do for the fulfilling of his sanctification.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to Aaron and his sons: Thus shall you bless the children of Israel, and you shall say to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord bless thee, and keep thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shew his face to thee, and have mercy on thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord turn his countenance to thee, and give thee peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall invoke my name upon the children of Israel, and I will bless them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass in the day that Moses had finished the tabernacle, and set it up, and had anointed and sanctified it with all its vessels, the altar likewise and all the vessels thereof,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The princes of Israel and the heads of the families, in every tribe, who were the rulers of them who had been numbered, offered

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their gifts before the Lord, six wagons covered, and twelve oxen. Two princes offered one wagon, and each one an ox, and they offered them before the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Receive them from them to serve in the ministry of the tabernacle, and thou shalt deliver them to the Levites according to the order of their ministry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore receiving the wagons and the oxen, delivered them to the Levites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Two wagons and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gerson, according to their necessity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The other four wagons, and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, according to their offices and service, under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to the sons of Caath he gave no wagons or oxen: because they serve in the sanctuary and carry their burdens upon their own shoulders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the princes offered for the dedication of the altar on the day when it was anointed, their oblation before the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Let each of the princes one day after another offer their gifts for the dedication of the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first day Nahasson the son of Aminadab of the tribe of Juda offered his offering:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his offering was a silver dish weighing one hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles according to the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A little mortar of ten sicles of gold full of incense:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An ox of the herd, and a ram, and lamb of a year old for a holocaust:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of a year old. This was the offering of Nahasson the son of Aminadab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The second day Nathanael the son of Suar, prince of the tribe of Issachar, made his offering,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A silver dish weighing one hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles, according to the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A little mortar of gold weighing ten sicles full of incense:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An ox of the herd, and a ram, and a lamb of a year old for a holocaust:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five buck goats, five lambs of a year old. This was the offering of Nathanael the son of Suar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The third day the prince of the sons of Zabulon, Eliab the son of Helon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered a silver dish weighing one hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles by the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five buck goats, five lambs of a year old. This is the oblation of Eliab the son of Helon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fourth day the prince of the sons of Ruben, Elisur the son of Sedeur,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered a silver dish weighing one hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles according to the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A little mortar of gold weighing ten sicles full of incense:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An ox of the herd, and a ram, and a lamb of a year old, for a holocaust:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for victims of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five buck goats, five lambs of a year old. This was the offering of Elisur the son of Sedeur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fifth day the prince of the sons of Simeon, Salamiel the son of Surisaddai,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered a silver dish weighing one hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles after the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

text: An ox of the herd, and a ram, and a lamb of a year old for a holocaust:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for sacrifices of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five buck goats, five lambs of a year old. This was the offering of Salamiel the son of Surisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sixth day the prince of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph the son of Duel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered a silver dish weighing a hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles by the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

text: A little mortar of gold weighing ten sicles full of incense:

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for sacrifices of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five buck goats, five lambs of a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph the son of Duel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The seventh day the prince of the sons of Ephraim, Elisama the son of Ammiud,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered a silver dish weighing a hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles according to the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A little mortar of gold weighing ten sicles full of incense:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An ox of the herd, and a ram, and a lamb of a year old for a holocaust:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The twelfth day the prince of the sons of Nephtali, Ahira the son of Enan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered a silver dish weighing a hundred and thirty sicles, a silver bowl of seventy sicles, according to the weight of the sanctuary, both full of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin:

text: And for sacrifices of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five buck goats, five lambs of a year old. This was the offering of Ahira the son of Enan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These were the offerings made by the princes of Israel in the dedication of the altar, in the day wherein it was consecrated. Twelve dishes of silver: twelve silver bowls: twelve little mortars of gold:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Each dish weighing a hundred and thirty sicles of silver, and each bowl seventy sicles: that is, putting all the vessels of silver together, two thousand four hundred sicles, by the weight of the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Twelve little mortars of gold full of incense, weighing ten sicles apiece, by the weight of the sanctuary: that is, in all a hundred and twenty sicles of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Twelve oxen out of the herd for a holocaust, twelve rams, twelve lambs of a year old, and their libations: twelve buck goats for sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for sacrifices of peace offerings, oxen twenty-four, rams sixty, buck goats sixty, lambs of a year old sixty. These things were offered in the dedication of the altar, when it was anointed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses entered into the tabernacle of the covenant, to consult the oracle, he heard the voice of one speaking to him from the propitiatory, that is over the ark between the two cherubims, and from this place he spoke to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to Aaron, and thou shalt say to him: When thou shalt place the seven lamps, let the candlestick be set up on the south side. Give orders therefore that the lamps look over against the north, towards the table of the loaves of proposition, over against that part shall they give light, towards which the candlestick looketh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron did so, and he put the lamps upon the candlestick, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now this was the work of the candlestick, it was of beaten gold, both the shaft in the middle, and all that came out of both sides of the branches: according to the pattern which the Lord had shewn to Moses, so he made the candlestick.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the Levites out of the midst of the children of Israel, and thou shalt purify them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to this rite: Let them be sprinkled with the water of purification, and let them shave all the hairs of their flesh. And when they shall have washed their garments, and are cleansed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall take an ox of the herd, and for the offering thereof fine flour tempered with oil: and thou shalt take another ox of the herd for a sin offering:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt bring the Levites before the tabernacle of the covenant, calling together all the multitude of the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Levites are before the Lord, the children of Israel shall put their hands upon them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron shall offer the Levites, as a gift in the sight of the Lord from the children of Israel, that they may serve in his ministry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Levites also shall put their hands upon the heads of the oxen, of which thou shalt sacrifice one for sin, and the other for a holocaust to the Lord, to pray for them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt set the Levites in the sight of Aaron and of his, and shalt consecrate them being offered to the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shalt separate them from the midst of the children of Israel, to be mine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And afterwards they shall enter into the tabernacle of the covenant, to serve me. And thus shalt thou purify and consecrate them for an oblation of the Lord: for as a gift they were given me by the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have taken them instead of the firstborn that open every womb in Israel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For all the firstborn of the children of Israel, both of men and of beasts, are mine. From the day that I slew every firstborn in the land of Egypt, have I sanctified them to myself:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I have taken the Levites for all the firstborn of the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And have delivered them for a gift to Aaron and his sons out of the midst of the people, to serve me for Israel in the tabernacle of the covenant, and to pray for them, lest there should be a plague among the people, if they should presume to approach unto my sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and Aaron and all the multitude of the children of Israel did with the Levites all that the Lord had commanded Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they were purified, and washed their garments. And Aaron lifted them up in the sight of the Lord, and prayed for them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That being purified they might go into the tabernacle of the covenant to do their services before Aaron and his sons. As the Lord had commanded Moses touching the Levites, so was it done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of the Levites: From twenty-five years old and upwards, they shall go in to minister in the tabernacle of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they shall have accomplished the fiftieth year of their age, they shall cease to serve:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be the ministers of their brethren in the tabernacle of the covenant, to keep the things that are committed to their care, but not to do the works. Thus shalt thou order the Levites touching their charge.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord spoke to Moses in the desert of Sinai, the second year after they were come out of the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the children of Israel make the phase in its due time,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fourteenth day of this month in the evening, according to all the ceremonies and justifications thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses commanded the children of Israel that they should make the phase.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they made it in its proper time: the fourteenth day of the month at evening, in mount Sinai. The children of Israel did according to all things that the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But behold some who were unclean by occasion of the soul of a man, who could not make the phase on that day, coming to Moses and Aaron,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Said to them: We are unclean by occasion of the soul of a man. Why are we kept back that we may not offer in its season the offering to the Lord among the children of Israel?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses answered them: Stay that I may consult the Lord what he will ordain concerning you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to the children of Israel: The man that shall be unclean by occasion of one that is dead, or shall be in a journey afar off in your nation, let him make the phase to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the second month, on the fourteenth day of the month in the evening, they shall eat it with unleavened bread and wild lettuce:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall not leave any thing thereof until morning, nor break a bone thereof, they shall observe all the ceremonies of the phase.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if any man is clean, and was not on a journey, and did not make the phase, that soul shall be cut off from among his people, because he offered not sacrifice to the Lord in due season: he shall bear his sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sojourner also and the stranger if they be among you, shall make the phase to the Lord according to the ceremonies and justifications thereof. The same ordinances shall be with you both for the stranger, and for him that was born in the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now on the day that the tabernacle was reared up, a cloud covered it. But from the evening there was over the tabernacle, as it were, the appearance of fire until the morning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So it was always: by day the cloud covered it, and by night as it were the appearance of fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the cloud that covered the tabernacle was taken up, then the children of Israel marched forward: and in the place where the cloud stood still, there they camped.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the commandment of the Lord they marched, and at his commandment they pitched the tabernacle. All the days that the cloud abode over the tabernacle, they remained in the same place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if it was so that it continued over it a long time, the children of Israel kept the watches of the Lord, and marched not,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as many days soever as the cloud stayed over the tabernacle. At the commandment of the Lord they pitched their tents, and at his commandment they took them down.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the cloud tarried from evening until morning, and immediately at break of day left the tabernacle, they marched forward: and if it departed after a day and a night, they took down their tents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if it remained over the tabernacle for two days or a month or a longer time, the children of Israel remained in the same place, and marched not: but immediately as soon as it departed, they removed the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the word of the Lord they pitched their tents, and by his word they marched: and kept the watches of the Lord according to his commandment by the hand of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Make thee two trumpets of beaten silver, wherewith thou mayest call together the multitude when the camp is to be removed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou shalt sound the trumpets, all the multitude shall gather unto thee to the door of the tabernacle of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou sound but once, the princes and the heads of the multitude of Israel shall come to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the sound of the trumpets be longer, and with interruptions, they that are on the east side, shall first go forward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And at the second sounding and like noise of the trumpet, they who lie on the south side shall take up their tents. And after this manner shall the rest do, when the trumpets shall sound for a march.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when the people is to be gathered together, the sound of the trumpets shall be plain, and they shall not make a broken sound.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Aaron the priest shall sound the trumpets: and this shall be an ordinance for ever in your generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you go forth to war out of your land against the enemies that fight against you, you shall sound aloud with the trumpets, and there shall be a remembrance of you before the Lord your God, that you may be delivered out of the hands of your enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If at any time you shall have a banquet, and on your festival days, and on the first days of your months, you shall sound the trumpets over the holocausts, and the sacrifices of peace offerings, that they may be to you for a remembrance of your God. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The second year, in the second month, the twentieth day of the month, the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel marched by their troops from the desert of Sinai, and the cloud rested in the wilderness of Pharan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
