text: And the first went forward according to the commandment of the Lord by the hand of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Juda by their troops: whose prince was Nahasson the son of Aminadab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the tribe of the sons of Issachar, the prince was Nathanael the son of Suar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the tribe of Zabulon, the prince was Eliab the son of Helon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tabernacle was taken down, and the sons of Gerson and Merari set forward, bearing it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Ruben also marched, by their troops and ranks, whose prince was Helisur the son of Sedeur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the tribe of Simeon, the prince was Salamiel the son of Surisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the tribe of Gad, the prince was Eliasaph the son of Duel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the Caathites also marched carrying the sanctuary. So long was the tabernacle carried, till they came to the place of setting it up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Ephraim also moved their camp by their troops, in whose army the prince was Elisama the son of Ammiud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the tribe of the sons of Manasses, the prince was Gamaliel the son of Phadassur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the tribe of Benjamin, the prince was Abidan the son of Gedeon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The last of all the camp marched the sons of Dan by their troops, in whose army the prince was Ahiezer the son of Ammisaddai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the tribe of the sons of Aser, the prince was Phegiel the son of Ochran.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the tribe of the sons of Nephtali, the prince was Ahira the son of Enan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This was the order of the camps, and marches of the children of Israel by their troops, when they set forward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to Hobab the son of Raguel the Madianite, his kinsman: We are going towards the place which the Lord will give us: come with us, that we may do thee good: for the Lord hath promised good things to Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answered him: I will not go with thee, but I will return to my country, wherein I was born.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Do not leave us: for thou knowest in what places we should encamp in the wilderness, and thou shalt be our guide.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thou comest with us, we will give thee what is the best of the riches which the Lord shall deliver to us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they marched from the mount of the Lord three days’ journey, and the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them, for three days providing a place for the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The cloud also of the Lord was over them by day when they marched.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the ark was lifted up, Moses said: Arise, O Lord, and let thy enemies be scattered, and let them that hate thee, flee from before thy face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when it was set down, he said: Return, O Lord, to the multitude of the host of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the mean time there arose a murmuring of the people against the Lord, as it were repining at their fatigue. And when the Lord heard it he was angry. And the fire of the Lord being kindled against them, devoured them that were at the uttermost part of the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the people cried to Moses, Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire was swallowed up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he called the name of that place, The burning: for that the fire of the Lord had been kindled against them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For a mixt multitude of people, that came up with them, burned with desire, sitting and weeping, the children of Israel also being joined with them, and said: Who shall give us flesh to eat?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt free cost: the cucumbers come into our mind, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlic.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Our soul is dry, our eyes behold nothing else but manna.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the manna was like coriander seed, of the colour of bdellium.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people went about, and gathering it, ground it in a mill, or beat it in a mortar, and boiled it in a pot, and made cakes thereof of the taste of bread tempered with oil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the dew fell in the night upon the camp, the manna also fell with it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Moses heard the people weeping by their families, every one at the door of his tent. And the wrath of the Lord was exceedingly enkindled: to Moses also the thing seemed insupportable.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to the Lord: Why hast thou afflicted thy servant? Wherefore do I not find favour before thee? And why hast thou laid the weight of all this people upon me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Have I conceived all this multitude, or begotten them, that thou shouldst say to me: Carry them in thy bosom as the nurse is wont to carry the little infant, and bear them into the land, for which thou hast sworn to their fathers?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whence should I have flesh to give to so great a multitude? They weep against me, saying: Give us flesh that we may eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am not able alone to bear all this people, because it is too heavy for me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if it seem unto thee otherwise, I beseech thee to kill me, and let me find grace in thy eyes, that I be not afflicted with so great evils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Gather unto me seventy men of the ancients of Israel, whom thou knowest to be ancients and masters of the people: and thou shalt bring them to the door of the tabernacle of the covenant, and shalt make them stand there with thee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That I may come down and speak with thee: and I will take of thy spirit, and will give to them, that they may bear with thee the burden of the people, and thou mayest not be burthened alone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say to the people: Be ye sanctified: to morrow you shall eat flesh: for I have heard you say: Who will give us flesh to eat? It was well with us in Egypt. That the Lord may give you flesh, and you may eat:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Not for one day, nor two, nor five, nor ten, no nor for twenty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But even for a month of days, till it come out at your nostrils, and become loathsome to you, because you have cast off the Lord, who is in the midst of you, and have wept before him, saying: Why came we out of Egypt?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said: There are six hundred thousand footmen of this people, and sayest thou: I will give them flesh to eat a whole month?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall then a multitude of sheep and oxen be killed, that it may suffice for their food? or shall the fishes of the sea be gathered together to fill them?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord answered him: Is the hand of the Lord unable? Thou shalt presently see whether my word shall come to pass or no.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore came, and told the people the words of the Lord, and assembled seventy men of the ancients of Israel, and made them to stand about the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord came down in a cloud, and spoke to him, taking away of the spirit that was in Moses, and giving to the seventy men. And when the spirit had rested on them they prophesied, nor did they cease afterwards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there remained in the camp two of the men, of whom one was called Eldad, and the other Medad, upon whom the spirit rested; for they also had been enrolled, but were not gone forth to the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they prophesied in the camp, there ran a young man, and told Moses, saying: Eldad and Medad prophesy in the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forthwith Josue the son of Nun, the minister of Moses, and chosen out of many, said: My lord Moses forbid them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he said: Why hast thou emulation for me? O that all the people might prophesy, and that the Lord would give them his spirit!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses returned, with the ancients of Israel, into the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a wind going out from the Lord, taking quails up beyond the sea brought them, and cast them into the camp for the space of one day’s journey, on every side of the camp round about, and they flew in the air two cubits high above the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The people therefore rising up all that day, and night, and the next day, gathered together of quails, he that did least, ten cores: and they dried them round about the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As yet the flesh was between their teeth, neither had that kind of meat failed: when behold the wrath of the Lord being provoked against the people, struck them with an exceeding great plague.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that place was called, The graves of lust: for there they buried the people that had lusted. And departing from the graves of lust, they came unto Haseroth, and abode there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Mary and Aaron spoke against Moses, because of his wife the Ethiopian,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said: Hath the Lord spoken by Moses only? Hath he not also spoken to us in like manner? And when the Lord heard this,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: (For Moses was a man exceeding meek above all men that dwelt upon earth)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Immediately he spoke to him, and to Aaron and Mary: Come out you three only to the tabernacle of the covenant. And when they were come out,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord came down in a pillar of the cloud, and stood in the entry of the tabernacle calling to Aaron and Mary. And when they were come,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to them: Hear my words: if there be among you a prophet of the Lord, I will appear to him in a vision, or I will speak to him in a dream.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But it is not so with my servant Moses who is most faithful in all my house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I speak to him mouth to mouth: and plainly, and not by riddles and figures doth he see the Lord. Why then were you not afraid to speak ill of my servant Moses?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And being angry with them he went away:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The cloud also that was over the tabernacle departed: and behold Mary appeared white as snow with a leprosy. And when Aaron had looked on her, and saw her all covered with leprosy,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to Moses: I beseech thee, my lord, lay not upon us this sin, which we have foolishly committed:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let her not be as one dead, and as an abortive that is cast forth from the mother’s womb. Lo, now one half of her flesh is consumed with the leprosy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses cried to the Lord, saying O God, I beseech thee heal her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord answered him: If her father had spitten upon her face, ought she not to have been ashamed for seven days at least? Let her be separated seven days without the camp, and afterwards she shall be called again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Mary therefore was put out of the camp seven days: and the people moved not from that place until Mary was called again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people marched from Haseroth, and pitched their tents in the desert of Pharan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there the Lord spoke to Moses, saying.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Send men to view the land of Chanaan, which I will give to the children of Israel, one of every tribe, of the rulers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses did what the Lord had commanded, sending from the desert of Pharan, principal men, whose names are these:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Ruben, Sammua the son of Zechur.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Simeon, Saphat the son of Huri.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Juda, Caleb the son of Jephone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Osee the son of Nun.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Phalti the son of Raphu.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Zabulon, Geddiel the son of Sodi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Joseph, of the sceptre of Manasses, Gaddi the son of Susi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Aser, Sthur the son of Michael.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Nephtali, Nahabi the son of Vapsi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Gad, Guel the son of Machi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the names of the men, whom Moses sent to view the land: and he called Osee the son of Nun, Josue.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses sent them to view the land of Chanaan, and said to them: Go you up by the south side. And when you shall come to the mountains,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: View the land, of what sort it is, and the people that are the inhabitants thereof, whether they be strong or weak: few in number or many:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land itself, whether it be good or bad: what manner of cities, walled or without walls:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ground, fat or barren, woody or without trees. Be of good courage, and bring us of the fruits of the land. Now it was the time when the firstripe grapes are fit to be eaten.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were gone up, they viewed the land from the desert of Sin, unto Rohob as you enter into Emath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they went up at the south side, and came to Hebron, where were Achiman and Sisai and Tholmai the sons of Enac. For Hebron was built seven years before Tanis the city of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going forward as far as the torrent of the cluster of grapes, they cut off a branch with its cluster of grapes, which two men carried upon a lever. They took also of the pomegranates and of the figs of that place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which was called Nehelescol, that is to say, the torrent of the cluster of grapes, because from thence the children of Israel had carried a cluster of grapes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that went to spy out the land returned after forty days, having gone round all the country,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And came to Moses and Aaron and to all the assembly of the children of Israel to the desert of Pharan, which is in Cades. And speaking to them and to all the multitude, they shewed them the fruits of the land:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they related and said: We came into the land to which thou sentest us, which in very deed floweth with milk and honey as may be known by these fruits:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But it hath very strong inhabitants, and the cities are great and walled. We saw there the race of Enac.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amalec dwelleth in the south, the Hethite and the Jebusite and the Amorrhite in the mountains: but the Chanaanite abideth by the sea and near the streams of the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the mean time Caleb, to still the murmuring of the people that rose against Moses, said: Let us go up and possess the land, for we shall be able to conquer it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the others, that had been with him, said: No, we are not able to go up to this people, because they are stronger than we.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they spoke ill of the land, which they had viewed, before the children of Israel, saying: The land which we have viewed, devoureth its inhabitants: the people, that we beheld are of a tall stature.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There we saw certain monsters of the sons of Enac, of the giant kind: in comparison of whom, we seemed like locusts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the whole multitude crying wept that night.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Would God that we had died in Egypt: and would God we may die in this vast wilderness, and that the Lord may not bring us into this land, lest we fall by the sword, and our wives and children be led away captives. Is it not better to return into Egypt?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said one to another: Let us appoint a captain, and let us return into Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses and Aaron heard this, they fell down flat upon the ground before the multitude of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Josue the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephone, who themselves also had viewed the land, rent their garments,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to all the multitude of the children of Israel: The land which we have gone round is very good:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the Lord be favourable, he will bring us into it, and give us a land flowing with milk and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be not rebellious against the Lord: and fear ye not the people of this land, for we are able to eat them up as bread. All aid is gone from them: the Lord is with us, fear ye not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when all the multitude cried out, and would have stoned them, the glory of the Lord appeared over the tabernacle of the covenant to all the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: How long will this people detract me? how long will they not believe me for all the signs that I have wrought before them?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will strike them therefore with pestilence, and will consume them: but thee I will make a ruler over a great nation, and a mightier than this is.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to the Lord: That the Egyptians, from the midst of whom thou hast brought forth this people,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the inhabitants of this land, (who have heard that thou, O Lord, art among this people, and art seen face to face, and thy cloud protecteth them, and thou goest before them in a pillar of a cloud by day, and in a pillar of fire by night,)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May hear that thou hast killed so great a multitude as it were one man and may say:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He could not bring the people into the land for which he had sworn, therefore did he kill them in the wilderness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let then the strength of the Lord be magnified, as thou hast sworn, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord is patient and full of mercy, by taking away iniquity and wickedness, and leaving no man clear, who visitest the sins of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forgive, I beseech thee, the sins of this people, according to the greatness of thy mercy, as thou hast been merciful to them from their going out of Egypt unto this place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: I have forgiven according to thy word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As I live: and the whole earth shall be filled with the glory of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But yet all the men that have seen my majesty, and the signs that I have done in Egypt, and in the wilderness, and have tempted me now ten times, and have not obeyed my voice,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall not see the land for which I swore to their fathers, neither shall any one of them that hath detracted me behold it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My servant Caleb, who being full of another spirit hath followed me, I will bring into this land which he hath gone round: and his seed shall possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Amalecite and the Chanaanite dwell in the valleys. To morrow remove the camp, and return into the wilderness by the way of the Red Sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How long doth this wicked multitude murmur against me? I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say therefore to them: As I live, saith the Lord: According as you have spoken in my hearing, so will I do to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the wilderness shall your carcasses lie. All you that were numbered from twenty years old and upward, and have murmured against me,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall not enter into the land, over which I lifted up my hand to make you dwell therein, except Caleb the son of Jephone, and Josue the son of Nun.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But your children, of whom you said, that they should be a prey to the enemies, will I bring in: that they may see the land which you have despised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your carcasses shall lie in the wilderness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your children shall wander in the desert forty years, and shall bear your fornication, until the carcasses of their fathers be consumed in the desert,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to the number of the forty days, wherein you viewed the land: a year shall be counted for a day. And forty years you shall receive your iniquities, and shall know my revenge:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as I have spoken, so will I do to all this wicked multitude, that hath risen up together against me: in this wilderness shall it faint away and die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore all the men, whom Moses had sent to view the land, and who at their return had made the whole multitude to murmur against him, speaking ill of the land that it was naught,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Died and were struck in the sight of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Josue the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephone lived, of all them that had gone to view the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses spoke all these words to all the children of Israel, and the people mourned exceedingly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold rising up very early in the morning, they went up to the top of the mountain, and said: We are ready to go up to the place, of which the Lord hath spoken: for we have sinned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to them: Why transgress you the word of the Lord, which shall not succeed prosperously with you?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go not up, for the Lord is not with you: lest you fall before your enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Amalecite and the Chanaanite are before you, and by their sword you shall fall, because you would not consent to the Lord, neither will the Lord be with you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they being blinded went up to the top of the mountain. But the ark of the testament of the Lord and Moses departed not from the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Amalecite came down, and the Chanaanite that dwelt in the mountain: and smiting and slaying them pursued them as far as Horma.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel and thou shalt say to them: When you shall be come unto the land of your habitation, which I will give you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall make an offering to the Lord, for a holocaust, or a victim, paying your vows, or voluntarily offering gifts, or in your solemnities burning a sweet savour unto the Lord, of oxen or of sheep:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever immolateth the victim, shall offer a sacrifice of fine flour, the tenth part of an ephi, tempered with the fourth part of a hin of oil:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall give the same measure of wine to pour out in libations for the holocaust or for the victim. For every lamb,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for every ram there shall be a sacrifice of flour of two tenths, which shall be tempered with the third part of a hin of oil:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall offer the third part the same measure of wine for the libation, for a sweet savour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when thou offerest a holocaust or sacrifice of oxen, to fulfil thy vow or for victims of peace offerings,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt give for every ox three tenths of flour tempered with half a hin of oil,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And wine for libations of the same measure, for an offering of most sweet savour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus shalt thou do

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For every ox and ram and lamb and kid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Both they that are born in the land, and the strangers

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall offer sacrifices after the same rite.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There shall be all one law and judgment both for you and for them who are strangers in the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you are come into the land which I will give you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall eat of the bread of that country, you shall separate firstfruits to the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the things you eat. As you separate firstfruits of your barnfloors:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So also shall you give firstfruits of your dough to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if through ignorance you omit any of these things, which the Lord hath spoken to Moses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And by him hath commanded you from the day that he began to command and thenceforward,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitude have forgotten to do it: they shall offer a calf out of the herd, a holocaust for a most sweet savour to the Lord, and the sacrifice and libations thereof, as the ceremonies require, and a buck goat for sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall pray for all the multitude of the children of Israel: and it shall be forgiven them, because they sinned ignorantly, offering notwithstanding a burnt offering to the Lord for themselves and for their sin and their Ignorance:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall be forgiven all the people of the children of Israel: and the strangers that sojourn among them: because it is the fault of all the people through ignorance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if one soul shall sin ignorantly, he shall offer a she goat of a year old for his sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall pray for him, because he sinned ignorantly before the Lord: and he shall obtain his pardon, and it shall be forgiven him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The same law shall be for all that sin by ignorance, whether they be natives or strangers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the soul that committeth any thing through pride, whether he be born in the land or a stranger (because he hath been rebellious against the Lord) shall be cut off from among his people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he hath contemned the word of the Lord, and made void his precept: therefore shall he be destroyed, and shall bear his iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass, when the children of Israel were in the wilderness, and had found a man gathering sticks on the sabbath day,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That they brought him to Moses and Aaron and the whole multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they put him into prison, not knowing what they should do with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Let that man die, let all the multitude stone him without the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had brought him out, they stoned him, and he died as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord also said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt tell them to make to themselves fringes in the corners of their garments, putting in them ribands of blue:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That when they shall see them, they may remember all the commandments of the Lord, and not follow their own thoughts and eyes going astray after divers things,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But rather being mindful of the precepts of the Lord, may do them and be holy to their God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that I might be your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold Core the son of Isaar, the son of Caath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiron the sons of Eliab, and Hon the son of Pheleth of the children of Ruben,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rose up against Moses, and with them two hundred and fifty others of the children of Israel, leading men of the synagogue, and who in the time of assembly were called by name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had stood up against Moses and Aaron, they said: Let it be enough for you, that all the multitude consisteth of holy ones, and the Lord is among them: Why lift you up yourselves above the people of the Lord?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When Moses heard this, he fell flat on his face:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And speaking to Core and all the multitude, he said: In the morning the Lord will make known who belong to him, and the holy he will join to himself: and whom he shall choose, they shall approach to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do this therefore: Take every man of you your censers, thou Core, and all thy company.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And putting fire in them to morrow, put incense upon it before the Lord: and whomsoever he shall choose, the same shall be holy: you take too much upon you, ye sons of Levi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said again to Core: Hear ye sons of Levi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is it a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath spared you from all the people, and joined you to himself, that you should serve him in the service of the tabernacle, and should stand before the congregation of the people, and should minister to him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Did he therefore make thee and all thy brethren the sons of Levi to approach unto him, that you should challenge to yourselves the priesthood also,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that all thy company should stand against the Lord? for what is Aaron that you murmur against him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiron the sons of Eliab. But they answered: We will not come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is it a small matter to thee, that thou hast brought us out of a land that flowed with milk and honey, to kill us in the desert, except thou rule also like a lord over us?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast brought us indeed into a land that floweth with rivers of milk and honey, and hast given us possessions of fields and vineyards; wilt thou also pull out our eyes? We will not come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore being very angry, said to the Lord: Respect not their sacrifices: thou knowest that I have not taken of them so much as a young ass at any time, nor have injured any of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to Core: Do thou and thy congregation stand apart before the Lord to morrow, and Aaron apart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take every one of you censers, and put incense upon them, offering to the Lord two hundred and fifty censers: let Aaron also hold his censer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When they had done this, Moses and Aaron standing,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And had drawn up all the multitude against them to the door of the tabernacle, the glory of the Lord appeared to them all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord speaking to Moses and Aaron, said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Separate yourselves from among this congregation, that I may presently destroy them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They fell flat on their face, and said: O most mighty, the God of the spirits of all flesh, for one man’s sin shall thy wrath rage against all?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the whole people to separate themselves from the tents of Core and Dathan and Abiron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses arose, and went to Dathan and Abiron: and the ancients of Israel following him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to the multitude: Depart from the tents of these wicked men, and touch nothing of theirs, lest you be involved in their sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were departed from their tents round about, Dathan and Abiron coming out stood in the entry of their pavilions with their wives and children, and all the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said: By this you shall know that the Lord hath sent me to do all things that you see, and that I have not forged them of my own head:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If these men die the common death of men, and if they be visited with a plague, wherewith others also are wont to be visited, the Lord did not send me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the Lord do a new thing, and the earth opening her mouth swallow them down, and all things that belong to them, and they go down alive into hell, you shall know that they have blasphemed the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And immediately as he had made an end of speaking, the earth broke asunder under their feet:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And opening her mouth, devoured them with their tents and all their substance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they went down alive into hell, the ground closing upon them, and they perished from among the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But all Israel, that was standing round about, fled at the cry of them that were perishing: saying: Lest perhaps the earth swallow us up also.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a fire coming out from the Lord, destroyed the two hundred and fifty men that offered the incense.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest to take up the censers that lie in the burning, and to scatter the fire of one side and the other: because they are sanctified

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the deaths of the sinners: and let him beat them into plates, and fasten them to the altar, because incense hath been offered in them to the Lord, and they are sanctified, that the children of Israel may see them for a sign and a memorial.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Eleazar the priest took the brazen censers, wherein they had offered, whom the burning fire had devoured, and beat them into plates, fastening them to the altar:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That the children of Israel might have for the time to come wherewith they should be admonished, that no stranger or any one that is not of the seed of Aaron should come near to offer incense to the Lord, lest he should suffer as Core suffered, and all his congregation, according as the Lord spoke to Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The following day all the multitude of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron, saying: You have killed the people of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when there arose a sedition, and the tumult increased,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses and Aaron fled to the tabernacle of the covenant. And when they were gone into it, the cloud covered it, and the glory of the Lord appeared.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Get you out from the midst of this multitude, this moment will I destroy them. And as they were lying on the ground,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses said to Aaron: Take the censer, and putting fire in it from the altar, put incense upon it, and go quickly to the people to pray for them: for already wrath is gone out from the Lord, and the plague rageth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When Aaron had done this, and had run to the midst of the multitude which the burning fire was now destroying, he offered the incense:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And standing between the dead and the living, he prayed for the people, and the plague ceased.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the number of them that were slain was fourteen thousand and seven hundred men, besides them that had perished in the sedition of Core.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron returned to Moses to the door of the tabernacle of the covenant after the destruction was over.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and take of every one of them a rod by their kindreds, of all the princes of the tribes, twelve rods, and write the name of every man upon his rod.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the name of Aaron shall be for the tribe of Levi, and one rod shall contain all their families:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt lay them up in the tabernacle of the covenant before the testimony, where I will speak to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whomsoever of these I shall choose, his rod shall blossom: and I will make to cease from me the murmurings of the children of Israel, wherewith they murmur against you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses spoke to the children of Israel: and all the princes gave him rods one for every tribe: and there were twelve rods besides the rod of Aaron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses had Laid them up before the Lord in the tabernacle of the testimony:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He returned on the following day, and found that the rod of Aaron for the house of Levi, was budded: and that the buds swelling it had bloomed blossoms, which spreading the leaves, were formed into almonds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore brought out all the rods from before the Lord to all the children of Israel: and they saw, and every one received their rods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Carry back the rod of Aaron into the tabernacle of the testimony, that it may be kept there for a token of the rebellious children of Israel, and that their complaints may cease from me lest they die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses did as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel said to Moses: Behold we are consumed, we all perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever approacheth to the tabernacle of the Lord, he dieth. Are we all to a man to be utterly destroyed?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Aaron: Thou, and thy sons, and thy father’s house with thee shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary: and thou and thy sons with thee shall bear the sins of your priesthood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And take with thee thy brethren also of the tribe of Levi, and the sceptre of thy father, and let them be ready in hand, and minister to thee: but thou and thy sons shall minister in the tabernacle of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Levites shall watch to do thy commands, and about all the works of the tabernacle: only they shall not come nigh the vessels of the sanctuary nor the altar, lest both they die, and you also perish with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But let them be with thee, and watch in the charge of the tabernacle, and in all the ceremonies thereof. A stranger shall not join himself with you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Watch ye in the charge of the sanctuary, and in the ministry of the altar: lest indignation rise upon the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have given you your brethren the Levites from among the children of Israel, and have delivered them for a gift to the Lord, to serve in the ministries of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou and thy sons look ye to the priesthood: and all things that pertain to the service of the altar, and that are within the veil, shall be executed by the priests. If any stranger shall approach, he shall be slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Aaron: Behold I have given thee the charge of my firstfruits. All things that are sanctified by the children of Israel, I have delivered to thee and to thy sons for the priestly office, by everlasting ordinances.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These therefore shalt thou take of the things that are sanctified, and are offered to the Lord. Every offering, and sacrifice, and whatsoever is rendered to me for sin and for trespass, and becometh holy of holies, shall be for thee and thy sons.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt eat it in the sanctuary: the males only shall eat thereof, because it is a consecrated thing to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the firstfruits, which the children of Israel shall vow and offer, I have given to thee, and to thy sons, and to thy daughters, by a perpetual law. He that is clean in thy house, shall eat them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the best of the oil, and of the wine, and of the corn, whatsoever firstfruits they offer to the Lord, I have given them to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the firstripe of the fruits, that the ground bringeth forth, and which are brought to the Lord, shall be for thy use: he that is clean in thy house, shall eat them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every thing that the children of Israel shall give by vow, shall be thine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whatsoever is firstborn of all flesh, which they offer to the Lord, whether it be of men, or of beasts, shall belong to thee: only for the firstborn of man thou shalt take a price, and every beast that is unclean thou shalt cause to be redeemed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the redemption of it shall be after one month, for five sicles of silver, by the weight of the sanctuary. A sicle hath twenty obols.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the firstling of a cow, and of a sheep and of a goat thou shalt not cause to be redeemed, because they are sanctified to the Lord. Their blood only thou shalt pour upon the altar, and their fat thou shalt burn for a most sweet odour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the flesh shall fall to thy use, as the consecrated breast, and the right shoulder shall be thine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the firstfruits of the sanctuary which the children of Israel offer to the Lord, I have given to thee and to thy sons and daughters, by a perpetual ordinance. It is a covenant of salt for ever before the Lord, to thee and to thy sons.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Aaron: You shall possess nothing in their land, neither shall you have a portion among them: I am thy portion and inheritance in the midst of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I have given to the sons of Levi all the tithes of Israel for a possession, for the ministry wherewith they serve me in the tabernacle of the covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That the children of Israel may not approach any more to the tabernacle, nor commit deadly sin,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But only the sons of Levi may serve me in the tabernacle, and bear the sins of the people. It shall be an everlasting ordinance in your generations. They shall not possess any other thing,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But be content with the oblation or tithes, which I have separated for their uses and necessities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the Levites, and declare unto them: When you shall receive of the children of Israel the tithes, which I have given you, offer the firstfruits of them to the Lord, that is to say, the tenth part of the tenth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it may be reckoned to you as an oblation of firstfruits, as well of the barnfloors as of the winepresses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of all the things of which you receive tithes, offer the firstfruits to the Lord, and give them to Aaron the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the things that you shall offer of the tithes, and shall separate for the gifts of the Lord, shall be the best and choicest things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say to them: If you offer all the goodly and the better things of the tithes, it shall be reckoned to you as if you had given the firstfruits of the barnfloor and the winepress:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall eat them in all your places, both you and your families: because it is your reward for the ministry, wherewith you serve in the tabernacle of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall not sin in this point, by reserving the choicest and fat things to yourselves, lest you profane the oblations of the children of Israel, and die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the observance of the victim, which the Lord hath ordained. Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee a red cow of full age, in which there is no blemish, and which hath not carried the yoke:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall deliver her to Eleazar the priest, who shall bring her forth without the camp, and shall immolate her in the sight of all:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And dipping his finger in her blood, shall sprinkle it over against the door of the tabernacle seven times,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall burn her in the sight of all delivering up to the fire her skin, and her flesh, and her blood, and her dung.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The priest shall also take cedar wood, and hyssop, and scarlet twice dyed, and cast it into the flame, with which the cow is consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then after washing his garments, and body, he shall enter into the camp, and shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He also that hath burned her, shall wash his garments, and his body, and shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a man that is clean shall gather up the ashes of the cow, and shall pour them forth without the camp in a most clean place, that they may be reserved for the multitude of the children of Israel, and for a water of aspersion: because the cow was burnt for sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he that carried the ashes of the cow, hath washed his garments, he shall be unclean until the evening. The children of Israel, and the strangers that dwell among them, shall observe this for a holy thing by a perpetual ordinance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that toucheth the corpse of a man, and is therefore unclean seven days,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall be sprinkled with this water on the third day, and on the seventh, and so shall be cleansed. If he were not sprinkled on the third day, he cannot be cleansed on the seventh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every one that toucheth the corpse of a man, and is not sprinkled with this mixture, shall profane the tabernacle of the Lord, and shall perish out of Israel: because he was not sprinkled with the water of expiation, he shall be unclean, and his uncleanness shall remain upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of a man that dieth in a tent: All that go into his tent and all the vessels that are there, shall be unclean seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The vessel that hath no cover, nor binding over it, shall be unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man in the field touch the corpse of a man that was slain, or that died of himself, or his bone, or his grave, he shall be unclean seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall take of the ashes of the burning and of the sin offering, and shall pour living waters upon them into a vessel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a man that is clean shall dip hyssop in them, and shall sprinkle therewith all the tent, and all the furniture, and the men that are defiled with touching any such thing:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in this manner he that is clean shall purify the unclean on the third and on the seventh day. And being expiated the seventh day, he shall wash both himself and his garments, and be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man be not expiated after this rite, his soul shall perish out of the midst of the church: because he hath profaned the sanctuary of the Lord, and was not sprinkled with the water of purification.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This precept shall be an ordinance for ever. He also that sprinkled the water, shall wash his garments. Every one that shall touch the waters of expiation, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whatsoever a person toucheth who is unclean, he shall make it unclean: and the person that toucheth any of these things, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel, and all the multitude came into the desert of Sin, in the first month: and the people abode in Cades. And Mary died there, and was buried in the same place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people wanting water, came together against Moses and Aaron:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And making a sedition, they said: Would God we had perished among our brethren before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why have you brought out the church of the Lord into the wilderness, that both we and our cattle should die?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why have you made us come up out of Egypt, and have brought us into this wretched place which cannot be sowed, nor bringeth forth figs, nor vines, nor pomegranates, neither is there any water to drink?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and Aaron leaving the multitude, went into the tabernacle of the covenant, and fell flat upon the ground, and cried to the Lord, and said. O Lord God, hear the cry of this people, and open to them thy treasure, a fountain of living water, that being satisfied, they may cease to murmur. And the glory of the Lord appeared over them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the rod, and assemble the people together, thou and Aaron thy brother, and speak to the rock before them, and it shall yield waters. And when thou hast brought forth water out of the rock, all the multitude and their cattle shall drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore took the rod, which was before the Lord, as he had commanded him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having gathered together the multitude before the rock, he said to them: Hear, ye rebellious and incredulous: Can we bring you forth water out of this rock?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses had lifted up his hand, and struck the rock twice with the rod, there came forth water in great abundance, so that the people and their cattle drank,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron: Because you have not believed me, to sanctify me before the children of Israel, you shall not bring these people into the land, which I will give them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the Water of contradiction, where the children of Israel strove with words against the Lord, and he was sanctified in them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the mean time Moses sent messengers from Cades to the king of Edom, to say: Thus saith thy brother Israel: Thou knowest all the labour that hath come upon us:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In what manner our fathers went down into Egypt, and there we dwelt a long time, and the Egyptians afflicted us and our fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And how we cried to the Lord, and he heard us, and sent an angel, who hath brought us out of Egypt. Lo, we are now in the city of Cades, which is in the uttermost of thy borders,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we beseech thee that we may have leave to pass through thy country. We will not go through the fields, nor through the vineyards, we will not drink the waters of thy wells, but we will go by the common highway, neither turning aside to the right hand, nor to the left, till we are past thy borders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Edom answered them: Thou shalt not pass by me: if thou dost I will come out armed against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel said: We will go by the beaten way: and if we and our cattle drink of thy waters, we will give thee what is just: there shall be no difficulty in the price, only let us pass speedily.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answered: Thou shalt not pass. And immediately he came forth to meet them with an infinite multitude, and a strong hand,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither would he condescend to their desire to grant them passage through his borders. Wherefore Israel turned another way from him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had removed the camp from Cades, they came to mount Hor, which is in the borders of the land of Edom:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where the Lord spoke to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let Aaron, saith he, go to his people: for he shall not go into the land which I have given the children of Israel, because he was incredulous to my words, at the waters of contradiction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take Aaron and his son with him, and bring them up into mount Hor:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou hast stripped the father of his vesture, thou shalt vest therewith Eleazar his son: Aaron shall be gathered to his people, and die there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses did as the Lord had commanded: and they went up into mount Hor before all the multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had stripped Aaron of his vestments, he vested Eleazar his son with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron being dead in the top of the mountain, he came down with Eleazar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the multitude seeing that Aaron was dead, mourned for him thirty days throughout all their families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when king Arad the Chanaanite, who dwelt towards the south, had heard this, to wit, that Israel was come by the way of the spies, he fought against them, and overcoming them carried off their spoils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Israel binding himself by vow to the Lord, said: If thou wilt deliver this people into my hand, I will utterly destroy their cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord heard the prayers of Israel, and delivered up the Chanaanite, and they cut them off and destroyed their cities: and they called the name of that place Horma, that is to say, Anathema.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they marched from mount Hor, by the way that leadeth to the Red Sea, to compass the land of Edom. And the people began to be weary of their journey and labour:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And speaking against God and Moses, they said: Why didst thou bring us out of Egypt, to die in the wilderness? There is no bread, nor have we any waters: our soul now loatheth this very light food.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore the Lord sent among the people fiery serpents, which bit them and killed many of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon which they came to Moses, and said; We have sinned, because we have spoken against the Lord and thee: pray that he may take away these serpents from us. And Moses prayed for the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to him: Make a brazen serpent, and set it up for a sign: whosoever being struck shall look on it, shall live.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore made a brazen serpent, and set it up for a sign: which when they that were bitten looked upon, they were healed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel setting forwards camped in Oboth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing thence they pitched their tents in Jeabarim, in the wilderness, that faceth Moab toward the east.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And removing from thence, they came to the torrent Zared:
