source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which they left and encamped over against Arnon, which is in the desert and standeth out on the borders of the Amorrhite. For Arnon is the border of Moab, dividing the Moabites and the Amorrhites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore it is said in the book of the wars of the Lord: As he did in the Red Sea, so will he do in the streams of Arnon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The rocks of the torrents were bowed down that they might rest in Ar, and lie down in the borders of the Moabites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When they went from that place, the well appeared whereof the Lord said to Moses: Gather the people together, and I will give them water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Israel sung this song: Let the well spring up. They sung thereto:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The well, which the princes dug, and the chiefs of the people prepared by the direction of the lawgiver, and with their staves. And they marched from the wilderness to Mathana.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From Mathana unto Nahaliel: from Nahaliel unto Bamoth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From Bamoth, is a valley in the country of Moab, to the top of Phasga, which looked towards the desert.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Israel sent messengers to Sehon king of the Amorrhites, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I beseech thee that I may have leave to pass through thy land: we will not go aside into the fields or the vineyards, we will not drink waters of the wells, we will go the king’s highway, till we be past thy borders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he would not grant that Israel should pass by his borders: but rather gathering an army, went forth to meet them in the desert, and came to Jasa and fought against them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was slain by them with the edge of the sword, and they possessed his land from the Arnon unto the Jeboc, and to the confines of the children of Ammon: for the borders of the Ammonites, were kept with a strong garrison.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Israel took all his cities, and dwelt in the cities of the Amorrhite, to wit, in Hesebon, and in the villages thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hesebon was the city of Sehon the king of the Amorrhites, who fought against the king of Moab: and took all the land, that had been of his dominion, as far as the Arnon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore it is said in the proverb: Come into Hesebon, let the city of Sehon be built and set up:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A fire is gone out of Hesebon, a flame from the city of Sehon, and hath consumed Ar of the Moabites, and the inhabitants of the high places of the Arnon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to thee Moab: thou art undone, O people of Chamos. He hath given his sons to flight, and his daughters into captivity to Sehon the king of the Amorrhites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their yoke is perished from Hesebon unto Dibon, they came weary to Nophe, and unto Medaba.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorrhite.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses sent some to take a view of Jazer: and they took the villages of it, and conquered the inhabitants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they turned themselves, and went up by the way of Basan, and Og the king of Basan came against them with all his people, to fight in Edrai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Fear him not, for I have delivered him and all his people, and his country into thy hand: and thou shalt do to him as thou didst to Sehon the king of the Amorrhites, the inhabitant of Hesebon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they slew him also with his sons, and all his people, not letting any one escape, and they possessed his land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they went forward and encamped in the plains of Moab, over against where Jericho is situate beyond the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balac the son of Sephor, seeing all that Israel had done to the Amorrhite,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that the Moabites were in great fear of him, and were not able to sustain his assault,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to the elders of Madian: So will this people destroy all that dwell in our borders, as the ox is wont to eat the grass to the very roots. Now he was at that time king in Moab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He sent therefore messengers to Balaam the son of Beor, a soothsayer, who dwelt by the river of the land of the children of Ammon, to call him, and to say: Behold a people is come out of Egypt, that hath covered the face of the earth, sitting over against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come therefore, and curse this people, because it is mightier than I: if by any means I may beat them and drive them out of my land: for I know that he whom thou shalt bless is blessed, and he whom thou shalt curse is cursed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ancients of Moab, and the elders of Madian, went with the price of divination in their hands. And where they were come to Balaam, and had told him all the words of Balac:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He answered: Tarry here this night and I will answer whatsoever the Lord shall say to me. And while they stayed with Balaam, God came and said to him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What mean these men that are with thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He answered: Balac the son of Sephor king of the Moabites hath sent to me,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Behold a people that is come out of Egypt, hath covered the face of the land: come and curse them, if by any means I may fight with them and drive them away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God said to Balaam: Thou shalt not go with them, nor shalt thou curse the people: because it is blessed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he rose in the morning and said to the princes: Go into your country, because the Lord hath forbid me to come with you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The princes returning, said to Balac: Balaam would not come with us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he sent many more and more noble than he had sent before:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who, when they were come to Balaam, said: Thus saith Balac the son of Sephor, Delay not to come to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I am ready to honour thee, and will give thee whatsoever thou wilt: come and curse this people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam answered: If Balac would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot alter the word of the Lord my God, to speak either more or less.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I pray you to stay here this night also, that I may know what the Lord will answer me once more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God therefore came to Balaam in the night, and said to him: If these men be come to call thee, arise and go with them: yet so, that thou do what I shall command thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam arose in the morning, and saddling his ass went with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And God was angry. And an angel of the Lord stood in the way against Balaam, who sat on the ass, and had two servants with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ass seeing the angel standing in the way, with a drawn sword, turned herself out of the way, and went into the field. And when Balaam beat her, and had a mind to bring her again to the way,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The angel stood in a narrow place between two walls, wherewith the vineyards were enclosed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ass seeing him, thrust herself close to the wall, and bruised the foot of the rider. But he beat her again:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And nevertheless the angel going on to a narrow place, where there was no way to turn aside either to the right hand or to the left, stood to meet him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the ass saw the angel standing, she fell under the feet of the rider: who being angry beat her sides more vehemently with a staff.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord opened the mouth of the ass, and she said: What have I done to thee? Why strikest thou me, lo, now this third time?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam answered: Because thou hast deserved it, and hast served me ill: I would I had a sword that I might kill thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ass said: Am not I thy beast, on which thou hast been always accustomed to ride until this present day? tell me if I ever did the like thing to thee. But he said: Never.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forthwith the Lord opened the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the angel standing in the way with a drawn sword, and he worshipped him falling flat on the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel said to him: Why beatest thou thy ass these three times? I am come to withstand thee, because thy way is perverse, and contrary to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And unless the ass had turned out of the way, giving place to me who stood against thee, I had slain thee, and she should have lived.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam said: I have sinned, not knowing that thou didst stand against me: and now if it displease thee that I go, I will return.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The angel said: Go with these men, and see thou speak no other thing than what I shall command thee. He went therefore with the princes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Balac heard it he came forth to meet him in a town of the Moabites, that is situate in the uttermost borders of Arnon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to Balaam: I sent messengers to call thee, why didst thou not come immediately to me? was it because I am not able to reward thy coming?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He answered him: Lo, here I am: shall I have power to speak any other thing but that which God shall put in my mouth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they went on together, and came into a city, that was in the uttermost borders of his kingdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Balac had killed oxen and sheep, he sent presents to Balaam, and to the princes that were with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when morning was come, he brought him to the high places of Baal, and he beheld the uttermost part of the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balaam said to Balac: Build me here seven altars, and prepare as many calves, and the same number of rams.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had done according to the word of Balaam, they laid together a calf and a ram upon every altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balaam said to Balac: Stand a while by thy burnt offering, until I go, to see if perhaps the Lord will meet me, and whatsoever he shall command, I will speak to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was gone with speed, God met him. And Balaam speaking to him, said: I have erected seven altars, and have laid on everyone a calf and a ram.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord put the word in his mouth, and said: Return to Balac, and thus shalt thou speak.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Returning he found Balac standing by his burnt offering, with all the princes of the Moabites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking up his parable, he said: Balac king of the Moabites hath brought me from Aram, from the mountains of the east: Come, said he, and curse Jacob: make haste and detest Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How shall I curse him, whom God hath not cursed? By what means should I detest him, whom the Lord detesteth not?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I shall see him from the tops of the rocks, and shall consider him from the hills. This people shall dwell alone, and shall not be reckoned among the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who can count the dust of Jacob, and know the number of the stock of Israel? Let my soul die the death of the just, and my last end be like to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balac said to Balaam: What is this that thou dost? I sent for thee to curse my enemies: and thou contrariwise blessest them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He answered him: Can I speak any thing else but what the Lord commandeth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balac therefore said: Come with me to another place from whence thou mayest see part of Israel, and canst not see them all: curse them from thence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had brought him to a high place, upon the top of mount Phasga, Balaam built seven altars, and laying on every one a calf and a ram,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to Balac: Stand here by thy burnt offering while I go to meet him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord had met him, and had put the word in his mouth, he said: Return to Balac, and thus shalt thou say to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Returning he found him standing by his burnt sacrifice, and the princes of the Moabites with him. And Balac said to him: What hath the Lord spoken?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he taking up his parable, said: Stand, O Balac, and give ear: hear, thou son of Sephor:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God is not a man, that he should lie, nor is the son of man, that he should be changed. Hath he said then, and will he not do? hath he spoken, and will he not fulfil?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was brought to bless, the blessing I am not able to hinder.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no idol in Jacob, neither is there an image god to be seen in Israel. The Lord his God is with him, and the sound of the victory of the king in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God hath brought him out of Egypt, whose strength is like to the rhinoceros.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no soothsaying in Jacob, nor divination in Israel. In their times it shall be told to Jacob and to Israel what God hath wrought.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the people shall rise up as a lioness, and shall lift itself up as a lion: it shall not lie down till it devour the prey, and drink the blood of the slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balac said to Balaam: Neither curse, nor bless him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Did I not tell thee, that whatsoever God should command me, that I would do?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balac said to him: Come and I will bring thee to another place; if peradventure it please God that thou mayest curse them from thence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had brought him upon the top of mount Phogor, which looketh towards the wilderness,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam said to him: Build me here seven altars, and prepare as many calves, and the same number of rams.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balac did as Balaam had said: and he laid on every altar, a calf and a ram.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Balaam saw that it pleased the Lord that he should bless Israel, he went not as he had gone before, to seek divination: but setting his face towards the desert,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And lifting up his eyes, he saw Israel abiding in their tents by their tribes: and the spirit of God rushing upon him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He took up his parable and said: Balaam the son of Beor hath said: The man hath said, whose eye is stopped up:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The hearer of the words of God hath said, he that hath beheld the vision of the Almighty, he that falleth, and so his eyes are opened:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How beautiful are thy tabernacles O Jacob, and thy tents, O Israel!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As woody valleys, as watered gardens near the rivers, as tabernacles which the Lord hath pitched, as cedars by the waterside.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Water shall flow out of his bucket, and his seed shall be in many waters. For Agag his king shall be removed, and his kingdom shall be taken away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God hath brought him out of Egypt, whose strength is like to the rhinoceros. They shall devour the nations that are his enemies, and break their bones, and pierce them with arrows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lying down he hath slept as a lion, and as a lioness, whom none shall dare to rouse. He that blesseth thee, shall also himself be blessed: he that curseth thee shall be reckoned accursed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balac being angry against Balaam, clapped his hands together and said: I called thee to curse my enemies, and thou on the contrary hast blessed them three times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Return to thy place. I had determined indeed greatly to honour thee, but the Lord hath deprived thee of the honour designed for thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam made answer to Balac: Did I not say to thy messengers, whom thou sentest to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If Balac would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the word of the Lord my God, to utter any thing of my own head either good or evil: but whatsoever the Lord shall say, that I will speak?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But yet going to my people, I will give thee counsel, what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore taking up his parable, again he said: Balaam the son of Beor hath said: The man whose eye is stopped up, hath said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The hearer of the words of God hath said, who knoweth the doctrine of the Highest, and seeth the visions of the Almighty, who falling hath his eyes opened:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I shall see him, but not now: I shall behold him, but not near. A STAR SHALL RISE out of Jacob and a sceptre shall spring up from Israel: and shall strike the chiefs of Moab, and shall waste all the children of Seth

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall possess Idumea: the inheritance of Seir shall come to their enemies, but Israel shall do manfully.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of Jacob shall he come that shall rule, and shall destroy the remains of the city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he saw Amalec, he took up his parable, and said: Amalec the beginning of nations, whose latter ends shall be destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saw also the Cinite: and took up his parable, and said: Thy habitation indeed is strong: but though thou build thy nest in a rock,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou be chosen of the stock of Cin, how long shalt thou be able to continue? For Assur shall take thee captive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking up his parable, again he said: Alas, who shall live when God shall do these things?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall come in galleys from Italy, they shall overcome the Assyrians, and shall waste the Hebrews, and at the last they themselves also shall perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balaam rose, and returned to his place: Balac also returned the way that he came.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Israel at that time abode in Settim, and the people committed fornication with the daughters of Moab,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who called them to their sacrifices. And they ate of them, and adored their gods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Israel was initiated to Beelphegor: upon which the Lord being angry,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Said to Moses: Take all the princes of the people, and hang them up on gibbets against the sun: that my fury may be turned away from Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to the judges of Israel: Let every man kill his neighbours, that have been initiated to Beelphegor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold one of the children of Israel went in before his brethren to a harlot of Madian, in the sight of Moses and of all the children of Israel, who were weeping before the door of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Phinees the son of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest saw it, he rose up from the midst of the multitude, and taking a dagger,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Went in after the Israelite into the brothel house, and thrust both of them through together, to wit, the man and the woman in the genital parts. And the scourge ceased from the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there were slain four and twenty thousand men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Phinees the son of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, hath turned away my wrath from the children of Israel: because he was moved with my zeal against them, that I myself might not destroy the children of Israel in my zeal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore say to him: behold I give him the peace of my covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the covenant of the priesthood for ever shall be both to him and his seed, because he hath been zealous for his God, and hath made atonement for the wickedness of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the name of the Israelite, that was slain with the woman of Madian, was Zambri the son of Salu, a prince of the kindred and tribe of Simeon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Madianite woman, that was slain with him, was called Cozbi the daughter of Sur, a most noble prince among the Madianites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the Madianites find you their enemies, and slay you them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because they also have acted like enemies against you, and have guilefully deceived you by the idol Phogor, and Cozbi their sister, a daughter of a prince of Madian, who was slain in the day of the plague for the sacrilege of Phogor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After the blood of the guilty was shed, the Lord said to Moses and to Eleazar the son of Aaron, the priest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Number the whole sum of the children of Israel from twenty years old and upward, by their houses and kindreds, all that are able to go forth to war.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore and Eleazar the priest, being in the plains of Moab upon the Jordan over against Jericho, spoke to them that were

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From twenty years old and upward, as the Lord had commanded: and this is the number of them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ruben the firstborn of Israel. His sons were Henoch, of whom is the family of the Henochites: and Phallu, of whom is the family of the Phalluites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Hesron, of whom is the family of the Hesronites: and Charmi, of whom is the family of the Charmites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the families of the stock of Ruben: whose number was found to be forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The son of Phallu was Eliab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His sons, were Namuel and Dathan and Abiron. These are Dathan and Abiron the princes of the people, that rose against Moses and Aaron in the sedition of Core, when they rebelled against the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the earth opening her mouth swallowed up Core, many others dying, when the fire burned two hundred and fifty men. And there was a great miracle wrought,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That when Core perished, his sons did not perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Simeon by their kindreds: Namuel, of him is the family of the Namuelites: Jamin, of him is the family of the Jaminites: Jachim, of him is the family of the Jachimites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Zare, of him is the family of the Zarites: Saul, of him is the family of the Saulites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the families of the stock of Simeon, of which the whole number was twenty-two thousand two hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Gad by their kindreds: Sephon, of him is the family of the Sephonites: Aggi, of him is the family of the Aggites: Suni, of him is the family of the Sunites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ozni, of him is the family of the Oznites: Her, of him is the family of the Herites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arod, of him is the family of the Arodites: Ariel, of him is the family of the Arielites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the families of Gad, of which the whole number was forty thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Juda, Her and Onan, who both died in the land of Chanaan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Juda by their kindreds were: Sela, of whom is the family of the Selaites: Phares, of whom is the family of the Pharesites: Zare, of whom is the family of the Zarites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover the sons of Phares were: Hesron, of whom is the family of the Hesronites: and Hamul, of whom is the family of the Hamulites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the families of Juda, of which the whole number was seventy-six thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Issachar, by their kindreds: Thola of whom is the family of the Tholaites: Phua, of whom is the family of the Phuaites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jasub, of whom is the family of the Jasubites: Semran, of whom is the family of the Semranites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the kindreds of Issachar, whose number was sixty-four thousand three hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Zabulon by their kindreds: Sared, of whom is the family of the Saredites: Elon, of whom is the family of the Elonites: Jalel, of whom is the family of the Jalelites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the kindreds of Zabulon, whose number was sixty thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Joseph by their kindred, Manasses and Ephraim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Manasses was born Machir, of whom is the family of the Machirites. Machir begot Galaad, of whom is the family of the Galaadites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Galaad had sons: Jezer, of whom is the family of the Jezerites: and Helec, of whom is the family of the Helecites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Asriel, of whom is the family of the Asrielites: and Sechem, of whom is the family of the Sechemites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Semida, of whom is the family of the Semidaites: and Hepher, of whom is the family of the Hepherites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Hepher was the father of Salphaad, who had no sons, but only daughters, whose names are these: Maala, and Noa, and Hegla, and Melcha, and Thersa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the families of Manasses, and the number of them fifty-two thousand seven hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Ephraim by their kindreds were these: Suthala, of whom is the family of the Suthalaites: Becher, of whom is the family of the Becherites: Thehen, of whom is the family of the Thehenites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the son of Suthala was Heran, of whom is the family of the Heranites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the kindreds of the sons of Ephraim: whose number was thirty-two thousand five hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the sons of Joseph by their families. The sons of Benjamin in their kindreds: Bela, of whom is the family of the Belaites: Asbel, of whom is the family of the Asbelites: Ahiram, of whom is the family of the Ahiramites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Supham, of whom is the family of the Suphamites: Hupham, of whom is the family of the Huphamites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Bela: Hered, and Noeman. Of Hered, is the family of the Heredites: of Noeman, the family of the Noemanites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the sons of Benjamin by their kindreds, whose number was forty-five thousand six hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Dan by their kindreds: Suham, of whom is the family of the Suhamites: These are the kindreds of Dan by their families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All were Suhamites, whose number was sixty-four thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Aser by their kindreds: Jemna, of whom is the family of the Jemnaites: Jessui, of whom is the family of the Jessuites: Brie, of whom is the family of the Brieites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Brie: Heber, of whom is the family of the Heberites: and Melchiel, of whom is the family of the Melchielites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the name of the daughter of Aser, was Sara.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the kindreds of the sons of Aser, and their number fifty-three thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sons of Nephtali by their kindreds: Jesiel, of whom is the family of the Jesielites: Guni, of whom is the family of the Gunites:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jeser, of whom is the family of the Jeserites: Sellem, of whom is the family of the Sellemites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the kindreds of the sons of Nephtali by their families: whose number was forty-five thousand four hundred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the sum of the children of Israel, that were reckoned up, six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To these shall the land be divided for their possessions according to the number of names.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To the greater number thou shalt give a greater portion, and to the fewer a less: to every one, as they have now been reckoned up, shall a possession be delivered:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet so that by lot the land be divided to the tribe and families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whatsoever shall fall by lot, that shall be taken by the more, or the fewer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This also is the number of the sons of Levi by their families: Gerson, of whom is the family of the Gersonites: Caath, of whom is the family of the Caathites: Merari, of whom is the family of the Merarites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the families of Levi: The family of Lobni, the family of Hebroni, the family of Moholi, the family of Musi, the family of Core. Now Caath begot Amram:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who had to wife Jochabed the daughter of Levi, who was born to him in Egypt. She bore to her husband Amram sons, Aaron and Moses, and Mary their sister.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of Aaron were born Nadab and Abiu, and Eleazar and Ithamar:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of whom Nadab and Abiu died, when they had offered the strange fire before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all that were numbered, were twenty-three thousand males from one month old and upward: for they were not reckoned up among the children of Israel, neither was a possession given to them with the rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the number of the children of Israel, that were enrolled by Moses and Eleazar the priest, in the plains of Moab upon the Jordan, over against Jericho.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Among whom there was not one of them that were numbered before by Moses and Aaron in the desert of Sinai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord had foretold that they should die in the wilderness. And none remained of them, but Caleb the son of Jephone, and Josue the son of Nun.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came the daughters of Salphaad, the son of Hepher, the son of Galaad, the son of Machir, the son of Manasses, who was the son of Joseph: and their names are Maala, and Noa, and Hegla, and Melcha, and Thersa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they stood before Moses and Eleazar the priest, and all the princes of the people at the door of the tabernacle of the covenant, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Our father died in the desert, and was not in the sedition, that was raised against the Lord under Core, but he died in his own sin: and he had no male children. Why is his name taken away out of his family, because he had no son? Give us a possession among the kinsmen of our father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses referred their cause to the judgment of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The daughters of Salphaad demand a just thing: Give them a possession among their father’s kindred, and let them succeed him in his inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the children of Israel thou shalt speak these things:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When a man dieth without a son, his inheritance shall pass to his daughter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he have no daughter, his brethren shall succeed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he have no brethren, you shall give the inheritance to his father’s brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he have no uncles by the father, the inheritance shall be given to them that are the next akin. And this shall be to the children of Israel sacred by a perpetual law, as the Lord hath commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord also said to Moses: Go up into this mountain Abarim, and view from thence the land which I will give to the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou shalt have seen it, thou also shalt go to thy people, as thy brother Aaron is gone:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because you offended me in the desert of Sin in the contradiction of the multitude, neither would you sanctify me before them at the waters. These are the waters of contradiction in Cades of the desert of Sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses answered him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May the Lord the God of the spirits of all flesh provide a man, that may be over this multitude:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And may go out and in before them, and may lead them out, or bring them in: lest the people of the Lord be as sheep without a shepherd.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to him: take Josue the son of Nun, a man in whom is the Spirit, and put thy hand upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall stand before Eleazar the priest and all the multitude:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt give him precepts in the sight of all, and part of thy glory, that all the congregation of the children of Israel may hear him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any thing be to be done, Eleazar the priest shall consult the Lord for him. He and all the children of Israel with him, and the rest of the multitude shall go out and go in at his word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses did as the Lord had commanded. And, when he had taken Josue, he set him before Eleazar the priest, and all the assembly of the people,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And laying his hands on his head, he repeated all things that the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord also said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: Offer ye my oblation and my bread, and burnt sacrifice of most sweet odour, in their due seasons.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the sacrifices which you shall offer: Two lambs of a year old without blemish every day for the perpetual holocaust:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One you shall offer in the mornings, and the other in the evening:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tenth part of an ephi of flour, which shall be tempered with the purest oil, of the measure of the fourth part of a hin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is the continual holocaust which you offered in mount Sinai for a most sweet odour of a sacrifice by fire to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for a libation you shall offer of wine the fourth part of a hin for every lamb in the sanctuary of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer the other lamb in like manner in the evening according to all the rites of the morning sacrifice, and of the libations thereof, an oblation of most sweet odour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the sabbath day you shall offer two lambs of a year old without blemish, and two tenths of flour tempered with oil in sacrifice, and the libations,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which regularly are poured out every sabbath for the perpetual holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the first day of the month you shall offer a holocaust to the Lord, two calves of the herd, one ram, and seven lambs of a year old, without blemish,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And three tenths of flour tempered with oil in sacrifice for every calf: and two tenths of flour tempered with oil for every ram:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tenth of a tenth of flour tempered with oil in sacrifice for every lamb. It is a holocaust of most sweet odour and an offering by fire to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these shall be the libations of wine that are to be poured out for every victim: Half a hin for every calf, a third for a ram, and a fourth for a lamb. This shall be the holocaust for every month, as they succeed one another in the course of the year.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A buck goat also shall be offered to the Lord for a sin offering over and above the perpetual holocaust with its libations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, shall be the phase of the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the fifteenth day the solemn feast: seven days shall they eat unleavened bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the first day of them shall be venerable and holy: you shall not do any servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer a burnt sacrifice a holocaust to the Lord, two calves of the herd, one ram, seven lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the sacrifice of every one three tenths of flour which shall be tempered with oil to every calf, and two tenths to every ram,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tenth of a tenth, to every lamb, that is to say, to all the seven lambs:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one buck goat for sin, to make atonement for you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Besides the morning holocaust which you shall always offer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shall you do every day of the seven days for the food of the fire, and for a most sweet odour to the Lord, which shall rise from the holocaust, and from the libations of each.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The seventh day also shall be most solemn and holy unto you, you shall do no servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The day also of firstfruits, when after the weeks are accomplished, you shall offer new fruits to the Lord, shall be venerable and holy: you shall do no servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer a holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, two calves of the herd, one ram, and seven lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the sacrifices of them three tenths of flour tempered with oil to every calf, two to every ram,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The tenth of a tenth to every lamb, which in all are seven lambs: a buck goat also,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which is slain for expiation: besides the perpetual holocaust and the libations thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall offer them all without blemish with their libations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first day also of the seventh month shall be venerable and holy unto you; you shall do no servile work therein, because it is the day of the sounding and of trumpets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer a holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, one calf of the herd, one ram and seven lambs of a year old, without blemish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for their sacrifices, three tenths of flour tempered with oil to every calf, two tenths to a ram,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One tenth to a lamb, which in all are seven lambs:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, which is offered for the expiation of the people,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Besides the holocaust of the first day of the month with the sacrifices thereof, and the perpetual holocaust with the accustomed libations. With the same ceremonies you shall offer a burnt sacrifice for a most sweet odour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The tenth day also of this seventh month shall be holy and venerable unto you, and you shall afflict your souls; you shall do no servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer a holocaust to the Lord for a most sweet odour, one calf of the herd, one ram, and seven lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for their sacrifices, three tenths of flour tempered with oil to every calf, two tenths to a ram,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The tenth of a tenth to every lamb, which are in all seven lambs:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the things that are wont to be offered for sin, for expiation, and for the perpetual holocaust with their sacrifice and libations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, which shall be unto you holy and venerable, you shall do no servile work, but shall celebrate a solemnity to the Lord seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer a holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, thirteen calves of the herd, two rams, and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for their libations three tenths of flour tempered with oil to every calf, being in all thirteen calves: and two tenths to each ram, being two rams,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tenth of a tenth to every lamb, being in all fourteen lambs:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On the second day you shall offer twelve calves of the herd, two rams and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations for every one, for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall duly celebrate:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for a sin offering besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The third day you shall offer eleven calves, two rams, and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations of every one for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall offer according to the rite:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice, and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fourth day you shall offer ten calves, two rams, and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations of every one for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall celebrate in right manner:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fifth day you shall offer nine calves, two rams, and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations of every one for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall celebrate according to the rite:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sixth day you shall offer eight calves, two rams, and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations of every one for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall celebrate according to the rite:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The seventh day you shall offer seven calves and two rams, and fourteen lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations of every one for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall celebrate according to the rite:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On the eighth day, which is most solemn, you shall do no servile work:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you shall offer a holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, one calf, one ram, and seven lambs of a year old, without blemish:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifices and the libations of every one for the calves and for the rams and for the lambs you shall celebrate according to the rite:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a buck goat for sin, besides the perpetual holocaust, and the sacrifice and the libation thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These things shall you offer to the Lord in your solemnities: besides your vows and voluntary oblations for holocaust, for sacrifice, for libation, and for victims of peace offerings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses told the children of Israel all that the Lord had commanded him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to the princes of the tribes of the children of Israel: This is the word that the Lord hath commanded:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man make a vow to the Lord, or bind himself by an oath: he shall not make his word void but shall fulfil all that he promised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a woman vow any thing, and bind herself by an oath, being in her father’s house, and but yet a girl in age: if her father knew the vow that she hath promised, and the oath wherewith she hath bound her soul, and held his peace, she shall be bound by the vow:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whatsoever she promised and swore, she shall fulfil in deed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if her father, immediately as soon as he heard it, gainsaid it, both her vows and her oaths shall be void, neither shall she be bound to what she promised, because her father hath gainsaid it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If she have a husband, and shall vow any thing, and the word once going out of her mouth shall bind her soul by an oath,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The day that her husband shall hear it, and not gainsay it, she shall be bound to the vow, and shall give whatsoever she promised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if as soon as he heareth he gainsay it, and make her promises and the words wherewith she had bound her soul of no effect: the Lord will forgive her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The widow, and she that is divorced, shall fulfil whatsoever they vow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the wife in the house of her husband, hath bound herself by vow and by oath,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If her husband hear, and hold his peace, and doth not disallow the promise, she shall accomplish whatsoever she had promised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if forthwith he gainsay it, she shall not be bound by the promise: because her husband gainsaid it, and the Lord will be merciful to her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If she vow and bind herself by oath, to afflict her soul by fasting, or abstinence from other things, it shall depend on the will of her husband, whether she shall do it, or not do it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the husband hearing it hold his peace, and defer the declaring his mind till another day: whatsoever she had vowed and promised, she shall fulfil: because immediately as he heard it, he held his peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he gainsay it after that he knew it, he shall bear her iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the laws which the Lord appointed to Moses between the husband and the wife, between the father and the daughter that is as yet but a girl in age, or that abideth in her father’s house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Revenge first the children of Israel on the Madianites, and so thou shalt be gathered to thy people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses forthwith said: Arm of you men to fight, who may take the revenge of the Lord on the Madianites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let a thousand men be chosen out of every tribe of Israel to be sent to the war.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they gave a thousand of every tribe, that is to say, twelve thousand men well appointed for battle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses sent them with Phinees the son of Eleazar the priest, and he delivered to him the holy vessels, and the trumpets to sound.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had fought against the Madianites and had overcome them, they slew all the men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their kings Evi, and Recem, and Sur, and Hur, and Rebe, five princes of the nation: Balaam also the son of Beor they killed with the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they took their women, and their children captives, and all their cattle, and all their goods: and all their possessions they plundered:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all their cities, and their villages, and castles, they burned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they carried away the booty, and all that they had taken both of men and of beasts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they brought them to Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and to all the multitude of the children of Israel. But the rest of the things for use they carried to the camp on the plains of Moab, beside the Jordan over against Jericho.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the princes of the synagogue went forth to meet them without the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses being angry with the chief officers of the army, the tribunes, and the centurions that were come from the battle,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Said: Why have you saved the women?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Are not these they, that deceived the children of Israel by the counsel of Balaam, and made you transgress against the Lord by the sin of Phogor, for which also the people was punished?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore kill all that are of the male sex, even of the children: and put to death the women, that have carnally known men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the girls, and all the women that are virgins save for yourselves:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And stay without the camp seven days. He that hath killed a man, or touched one that is killed, shall be purified the third day and the seventh day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of all the spoil, every garment, or vessel, or any thing made for use, of the skins, or hair of goats, or of wood, shall be purified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Eleazar also the priest spoke to the men of the army, that had fought, in this manner: This is the ordinance of the law, which the Lord hath commanded Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gold, and silver, and brass, and iron, and lead, and tin,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all that may pass through the fire, shall be purified by fire, but whatsoever cannot abide the fire, shall be sanctified with the water of expiation:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall wash your garments the seventh day, and being purified, you shall afterwards enter into the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take the sum of the things that were taken both of man and beast, thou and Eleazar the priest and the princes of the multitude:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt divide the spoil equally, between them that fought and went out to the war, and between the rest of the multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt separate a portion to the Lord from them that fought and were in the battle, one soul of five hundred as well of persons as of oxen and asses and sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt give it to Eleazar the priest, because they are the firstfruits of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of the moiety also of the children of Israel thou shalt take the fiftieth head of persons, and of oxen, and asses, and sheep, and of all beasts, and thou shalt give them to the Levites that watch in the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and Eleazar did as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the spoil which the army had taken, was six hundred seventy-five thousand sheep,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seventy-two thousand oxen,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sixty-one thousand asses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thirty-two thousand persons of the female sex, that had not known men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one half was given to them that had been in the battle, to wit, three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of which, for the portion of the Lord, were reckoned six hundred seventy five sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And out of the thirty-six thousand oxen, seventy-two oxen:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of the thirty thousand five hundred asses, sixty-one asses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of the sixteen thousand persons, there fell to the portion of the Lord, thirty-two souls.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses delivered the number of the firstfruits of the Lord to Eleazar the priest, as had been commanded him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of the half of the children of Israel, which he had separated for them that had been in the battle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But out of the half that fell to the rest of the multitude, that is to say, out of the three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And out of the thirty-six thousand oxen,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And out of the thirty thousand five hundred asses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And out of the sixteen thousand persons,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses took the fiftieth head, and gave it to the Levites that watched in the tabernacle of the Lord, as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the commanders of the army, and the tribunes and centurions were come to Moses, they said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We thy servants have reckoned up the number of the fighting men, whom we had under our hand, and not so much as one was wanting.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore we offer as gifts to the Lord what gold every one of us could find in the booty, in garters and tablets, rings and bracelets, and chains, that thou mayst pray to the Lord for us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and Eleazar the priest received all the gold in divers kinds,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In weight sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty sicles, from the tribunes and from the centurions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For that which every one had taken in the booty was his own.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that which was received they brought into the tabernacle of the testimony, for a memorial of the children of Israel before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Ruben and Gad had many flocks of cattle, and their substance in beasts was infinite. And when they saw the lands of Jazer and Galaad fit for feeding cattle,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
