text: They came to Moses and Eleazar the priest, and the princes of the multitude, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nemra, Hesebon, and Eleale, and Saban, and Nebo, and Beon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land, which the Lord hath conquered in the sight of the children of Israel, is a very fertile soil for the feeding of beasts: and we thy servants have very much cattle:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we pray thee, if we have found favour in thy sight, that thou give it to us thy servants in possession, and make us not pass over the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses answered them: What, shall your brethren go to fight, and will you sit here?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why do ye overturn the minds of the children of Israel, that they may not dare to pass into the place which the Lord hath given them?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Was it not thus your fathers did, when I sent from Cadesbarne to view the land?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were come as far as the valley of the cluster, having viewed all the country, they overturned the hearts of the children of Israel, that they should not enter into the coasts, which the Lord gave them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he swore in his anger, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If these men, that came up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land, which I promised with an oath to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: because they would not follow me,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Except Caleb the son of Jephone the Cenezite, and Josue the son of Nun: these have fulfilled my will.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord being angry against Israel, led them about through the desert forty years, until the whole generation, that had done evil in his sight, was consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold, said he, you are risen up instead of your fathers, the increase and offspring of sinful men, to augment the fury of the Lord against Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if you will not follow him, he will leave the people in the wilderness, and you shall be the cause of the destruction of all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they coming near, said: We will make sheepfolds, and stalls for our cattle, and strong cities for our children:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we ourselves will go armed and ready for battle before the children of Israel, until we bring them in unto their places. Our little ones, and all we have, shall be in walled cities, for fear of the ambushes of the inhabitants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We will not return into our houses until the children of Israel possess their inheritance:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither will we seek any thing beyond the Jordan, because we have already our possession on the east side thereof,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to them: If you do what you promise, go on well appointed for war before the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And let every fighting man pass over the Jordan, until the Lord overthrow his enemies:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the land be brought under him, then shall you be blameless before the Lord and before Israel, and you shall obtain the countries that you desire, before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you do not what you say, no man can doubt but you sin against God: and know ye, that your sin shall overtake you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Build therefore cities for your children, and folds and stalls for your sheep and beasts, and accomplish what you have promised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Gad and Ruben said to Moses: We are thy servants, we will do what my lord commandeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We will leave our children, and our wives and sheep and cattle, in the cities of Galaad:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we thy servants all well appointed will march on to the war, as thou, my lord, speakest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore commanded Eleazar the priest, and Josue the son of Nun, and the princes of the families of all the tribes of Israel, and said to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the children of Gad, and the children of Ruben pass with you over the Jordan, all armed for war before the Lord, and the land be made subject to you: give them Galaad in possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if they will not pass armed with you into the land of Chanaan, let them receive places to dwell in among you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Gad, and the children of Ruben answered: As the Lord hath spoken to his servants, so will we do:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We will go armed before the Lord into the land of Chanaan, and we confess that we have already received our possession beyond the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore gave to the children of Gad and of Ruben, and to the half tribe of Manasses the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sehon king of the Amorrhites, and the kingdom of Og king of Basan, and their land and the cities thereof round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Etroth, and Sophan, and Jazer, and Jegbaa,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Bethnemra, and Betharan, fenced cities, and folds for their cattle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the children of Ruben built Hesebon, and Eleale, and Cariathaim,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Nabo, and Baalmeon (their names being changed) and Sabama: giving names to the cities which they had built.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover the children of Machir, the son of Manasses, went into Galaad, and wasted it, cutting off the Amorrhites, the inhabitants thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses gave the land of Galaad to Machir the son of Manasses, and he dwelt in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jair the son of Manasses went, and took the villages thereof, and he called them Havoth Jair, that is to say, the villages of Jair.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nobe also went, and took Canath with the villages thereof: and he called it by his own name, Nobe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the mansions of the children of Israel, who went out of Egypt by their troops under the conduct of Moses and Aaron,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which Moses wrote down according to the places of their encamping, which they changed by the commandment of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the children of Israel departed from Ramesses the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month, the day after the phase, with a mighty hand, in the sight of all the Egyptians,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who were burying their firstborn, whom the Lord had slain (upon their gods also he had executed vengeance,)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they camped in Soccoth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from Soccoth they came into Etham, which is in the uttermost borders of the wilderness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Departing from thence they came over against Phihahiroth, which looketh towards Beelsephon, and they camped before Magdalum.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Phihahiroth, they passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness: and having marched three days through the desert of Etham, they camped in Mara.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Mara, they came into Elim, where there were twelve fountains of waters, and seventy palm trees: and there they camped.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But departing from thence also, they pitched their tents by the Red Sea. And departing from the Red Sea,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They camped in the desert of Sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they removed from thence, and came to Daphca.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Daphca, they camped in Alus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Alus, they pitched their tents in Raphidim, where the people wanted water to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Raphidim, they camped in the desert of Sinai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But departing also from the desert of Sinai, they came to the graves of lust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from the graves of lust, they camped in Haseroth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from Haseroth they came to Rethma.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Rethma, they camped in Remmomphares.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they departed from thence and came to Lebna.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Removing from Lebna they camped in Ressa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Ressa, they came to Ceelatha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they removed from thence and camped in the mountain Sepher.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Departing from the mountain Sepher, they came to Arada,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thence they went and camped in Maceloth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Maceloth, they came to Thahath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Removing from Thahath they camped in Thare.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they departed from thence, and pitched their tents in Methca.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And removing from Methca, they camped in Hesmona.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Hesmona, they came to Moseroth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And removing from Moseroth, they camped in Benejaacan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Benejaacan, they came to mount Gadgad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thence they went and camped in Jetebatha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from Jetebatha they came to Hebrona.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Hebrona, they camped in Asiongaber.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They removed from thence and came into the desert of Sin, which is Cades.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Cades, they camped in mount Hor, in the uttermost borders of the land of Edom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron the priest went up into mount Hor at the commandment of the Lord: and there he died in the fortieth year of the coming forth of the children of Israel out of Egypt, the fifth month, the first day of the month,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When he was a hundred and twenty-three years old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And king Arad the Chanaanite, who dwelt towards the south, heard that the children of Israel were come to the land of Chanaan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they departed from mount Hor, and camped in Salmona.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From whence they removed and came to Phunon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Phunon, they camped in Oboth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from Oboth they came to Ijeabarim, which is in the borders of the Moabites.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Ijeabarim they pitched their tents in Dibongab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thence they went and camped in Helmondeblathaim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Helmondeblathaim, they came to the mountains of Abarim over against Nabo.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from the mountains of Abarim, they passed to the plains of Moab, by the Jordan, over against Jericho.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there they camped from Bethsimoth even to Ablesatim in the plains of the Moabites,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel, and say to them: When you shall have passed over the Jordan, entering into the land of Chanaan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Destroy all the inhabitants of that land: Beat down their pillars, and break in pieces their statues, and waste all their high places,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cleansing the land, and dwelling in it. For I have given it you for a possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall divide it among you by lot. To the more you shall give a larger part, and to the fewer a lesser. To every one as the lot shall fall, so shall the inheritance be given. The possession shall be divided by the tribes and the families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will not kill the inhabitants of the land: they that remain, shall be unto you as nails in your eyes, and spears in your sides, and they shall be your adversaries in the land of your habitation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whatsoever I had thought to do to them, I will do to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you are entered into the land of Chanaan, and it shall be fallen into your possession by lot, it shall be bounded by these limits:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The south side shall begin from the wilderness of Sin, which is by Edom: and shall have the most salt sea for its furthest limits eastward:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which limits shall go round on the south side by the ascent of the Scorpion and so into Senna, and reach toward the south as far as Cadesbarne, from whence the frontiers shall go out to the town called Adar, and shall reach as far as Asemona.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the limits shall fetch a compass from Asemona to the torrent of Egypt, and shall end in the shore of the great sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the west side shall begin from the great sea, and the same shall be the end thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But toward the north side the borders shall begin from the great sea, reaching to the most high mountain,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From which they shall come to Emath, as far as the borders of Sedada:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the limits shall go as far as Zephrona, and the village of Enan. These shall be the borders on the north side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thence they shall mark out the bounds towards the east side from the village of Enan unto Sephama.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from Sephama the bounds shall go down to Rebla over against the fountain of Daphnis: from thence they shall come eastward to the sea of Cenereth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall reach as far as the Jordan, and at the last shall be closed in by the most salt sea. This shall be your land with its borders round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying: This shall be the land which you shall possess by lot, and which the Lord hath commanded to be given to the nine tribes, and to the half tribe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the tribe of the children of Ruben by their families, and the tribe of the children of Gad according to the number of their kindreds, and half of the tribe of Manasses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That is, two tribes and a half, have received their portion beyond the Jordan over against Jericho at the east side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the names of the men, that shall divide the land unto you: Eleazar the priest, and Josue the son of Nun,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one prince of every tribe,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whose names are these: Of the tribe of Juda, Caleb the son of Jephone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Simeon, Samuel the son of Ammiud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad the son of Chaselon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of the children of Dan, Bocci the son of Jogli.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the children of Joseph of the tribe of Manasses, Hanniel the son of Ephod.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Camuel the son of Sephtan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Zabulon, Elisaphan the son of Pharnach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Issachar, Phaltiel the prince, the son of Ozan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Aser, Ahiud the son of Salomi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Nephtali: Phedael the son of Ammiud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are they Whom the Lord hath commanded to divide the land of Chanaan to the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke these things also to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan, over against Jericho:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel that they give to the Levites out of their possessions,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cities to dwell in, and their suburbs round about: that they may abide in the towns, and the suburbs may be for their cattle and beasts:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which suburbs shall reach from the walls of the cities outward, a thousand paces on every side:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Toward the east shall be two thousand cubits: and toward the south in like manner shall be two thousand cubits: toward the sea also, which looketh to the west, shall be the same extent: and the north side shall be bounded with the like limits. And the cities shall be in the midst, and the suburbs without.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And among the cities, which you shall give to the Levites, six shall be separated for refuge to fugitives, that he who hath shed blood may flee to them: and besides these there shall be other forty-two cities,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That is, in all forty-eight with their suburbs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of these cities which shall be given out of the possessions of the children of Israel, from them that have more, more shall be taken: and from them that have less, fewer. Each shall give towns to the Levites according to the extent of their inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord said to Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you shall have passed over the Jordan into the land of Chanaan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Determine what cities shall be for the refuge of fugitives, who have shed blood against their will.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the fugitive shall be in them, the kinsman of him that is slain may not have power to kill him, until he stand before the multitude, and his cause be judged.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of those cities, that are separated for the refuge of fugitives,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Three shall be beyond the Jordan, and three in the land of Chanaan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As well for the children of Israel as for strangers and sojourners, that he may flee to them, who hath shed blood against his will.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man strike with iron, and he die that was struck: he shall be guilty of murder, and he himself shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he throw a stone, and he that is struck die: he shall be punished in the same manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he that is struck with wood die: he shall be revenged by the blood of him that struck him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The kinsman of him that was slain, shall kill the murderer: as soon as he apprehendeth him, he shall kill him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If through hatred any one push a man, or fling any thing at him with ill design:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or being his enemy, strike him with his hand, and he die: the striker shall be guilty of murder: the kinsman of him that was slain as soon as he findeth him, shall kill him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if by chance medley, and without hatred,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And enmity, he do any of these things,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this be proved in the hearing of the people, and the cause be debated between him that struck, and the next of kin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The innocent shall be delivered from the hand of the revenger, and shall be brought back by sentence into the city, to which he had fled, and he shall abide there until the death of the high priest, that is anointed with the holy oil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the murderer be found without the limits of the cities that are appointed for the banished,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And be struck by him that is the avenger of blood: he shall not be guilty that killed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the fugitive ought to have stayed in the city until the death of the high priest: and after he is dead, then shall the manslayer return to his own country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These things shall be perpetual, and for an ordinance in all your dwellings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The murderer shall be punished by witnesses: none shall be condemned upon the evidence of one man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not take money of him that is guilty of blood, but he shall die forthwith.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The banished and fugitives before the death of the high priest may by no means return into their own cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Defile not the land of your habitation, which is stained with the blood of the innocent: neither can it otherwise be expiated, but by his blood that hath shed the blood of another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thus shall your possession be cleansed, myself abiding with you. For I am the Lord that dwell among the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the princes of the families of Galaad, the son of Machir, the son of Manasses, of the stock of the children of Joseph, came and spoke to Moses before the princes of Israel, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath commanded thee, my lord, that thou shouldst divide the land by lot to the children of Israel, and that thou shouldst give to the daughters of Salphaad our brother the possession due to their father:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now if men of another tribe take them to wives, their possession will follow them, and being transferred to another tribe, will be a diminishing of our inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And so it shall come to pass, that when the jubilee, that is, the fiftieth year of remission, is come, the distribution made by the lots shall be confounded, and the possession of the one shall pass to the others.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses answered the children of Israel, and said by the command of the Lord: The tribe of the children of Joseph hath spoken rightly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this is the law promulgated by the Lord touching the daughters of Salphaad: Let them marry to whom they will, only so that it be to men of their own tribe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest the possession of the children of Israel be mingled from tribe to tribe. For all men shall marry wives of their own tribe and kindred:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all women shall take husbands of the same tribe: that the inheritance may remain in the families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that the tribes be not mingled one with another, but remain so

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As they were separated by the Lord. And the daughters of Salphaad did as was commanded:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Maala, and Thersa, and Hegla, and Melcha, and Noa were married to the sons of their uncle by their father

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the family of Manasses, who was the son of Joseph: and the possession that had been allotted to them, remained in the tribe and family of their father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the commandments and judgment, which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses to the children of Israel, in the plains of Moab upon the Jordan over against Jericho.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
